- **rf2-hbt1 data-direct contract** (qppo). Tests covering the contract surface?
- **rf2-piag render-key tuple** (t5tx). Tests covering the tuple shape and rendering?
- **rf2-een2 on-spawn data-relative** (smba). Tests covering `:on-spawn` callback receiving `(data, spawned-id)`, mutating data correctly, parent's data updated atomically?
- **rf2-xb5o event-arg split** (pz9f). Tests for the new arg shape?
- **rf2-z7f7 source-coord DOM annotation** (PR #135). Agent reported: 5 new SSR source-coord tests + 9 new CLJS DOM-injection tests + 18 sentinels in elision probe. Coverage: Form-1 capture, Form-2 wrapping, Fragment/interop fallback warning, production-elision, server-side rendering. Does the test suite cover all of these? Does the warning fire-once-per-id semantics have a test?

### Infra / process
- **rf2-0hxm substrate artefact split**. Bundle-isolation tests for the substrate artefact?
- **rf2-ace2 CI/CD restructure** (PR #134). Lockstep verifier has positive + negative tests. The topological deploy DAG itself is not exercisable without running an actual release — accept that and move on.
- **rf2-gqm7 examples restructure** (PR #131). Paths sweep, examples passing in CI. No new behaviour to test beyond the existing examples suite.
- **rf2-fc8i Information for AIs** (README). Documentation only, no tests needed.
- **rf2-7j0q namespace re-frame.core**. Test coverage assumed via existing core tests, but verify.
- **rf2-tbli alpha disposition**. Documentation/decision; verify no behaviour change snuck in.

## Process

For each item above:
1. Read the PR diff (`gh pr view <N> --json files,body`).
2. Identify the new behaviour added.
3. Check: is there a test in the diff (or a follow-up commit) that exercises this behaviour with assertions on the documented contract?
4. If yes — note "covered, [count] tests".
5. If no or partial — flag as a gap with sized recommendation:
   - Trivial gap (one missing case) → fold into a single follow-up bead "test gaps from rf2-XXXX..rf2-YYYY sweep".
   - Substantial gap (whole feature lacks coverage) → file a per-feature gap bead.

## Deliverable

Write findings to: `C:\Users\miket\code\re-frame2\findings\test-coverage-audit-2026-05-09.md`.

Findings doc format (~1500-2500 words):
- §1 Methodology — what was checked, what was excluded.
- §2 Coverage matrix — table mapping each PR → new behaviour → test status (covered / partial / missing).
- §3 Gap list — each gap sized + recommended bead title.
- §4 Top recommendations — which 3 gaps are most load-bearing.

Then post-back report (under 250 words):
1. Path to findings file.
2. Total gaps count + load-bearing count.
3. Top 3 gap-bead titles.
4. Approximate word count.

Do NOT file the gap-beads yet — Mike reviews findings and decides which to file (mirror the rf2-nfyf pattern).
Do NOT modify code. Do NOT branch. Do NOT open a PR.

## Disposition

P2 — pre-beta verification. Ideally completes before any further per-feature splits land so the test-coverage convention catches up to the impl velocity.
notes: 2026-05-09 bookkeeping: top-3 + sweep beads filed and merged: rf2-51x5 bundle-isolation CI gate (PR #149), rf2-5b6x late-bind missing-artefact coverage (PR #148), rf2-1e0n 3-arity guard coverage (PR #156), rf2-d4v7 reg-view test gaps sweep (PR #153), rf2-22ds frame-provider runtime React-context coverage (deferred). Audit doc itself remains open as reference material; close when Mike confirms all derived beads have been dispatched.
close_reason: Audit complete. Top-5 recommendations all actioned: rf2-51x5 (bundle-isolation CI gate, PR #149), rf2-5b6x (late-bind missing-artefact coverage, PR #148), rf2-1e0n (3-arity guard coverage, PR #156), rf2-d4v7 (reg-view test gaps sweep, PR #153), rf2-22ds (deferred 30d, blocked-by rf2-kdwc). Findings doc remains as reference at findings/test-coverage-audit-2026-05-09.md.

title: Implement Option B: :system-id named-machine addressing + finish spec 005 spawn-fx live-handler wiring (resolves rf2-ecv4)
description:
## Decision (from rf2-ecv4)

Adopt Option B: opt-in `:system-id` on `:invoke` / `[:spawn ...]` plus `(rf/machine-by-system-id sid)` lens. Decline Option C (hierarchical system primitive).

## Scope

### Surface additions

| Surface | Owner |
|---|---|
| `:system-id` key on `:invoke` / `[:spawn ...]` spec | spec 005 |
| `[:rf/system-ids]` reserved app-db slot | spec 002 (alongside `[:rf/machines]`) |
| `(rf/machine-by-system-id sid)` lens | spec 005 §Querying machines |
| Optional `(rf/dispatch-to-system sid event)` sugar | spec 005 |
| Trace event `:rf.machine/system-id-bound` (and `-released`) | spec 009 |
| Error category `:rf.error/system-id-collision` | spec 009 |
| Late-bind hook `:machines/machine-by-system-id` | core late-bind |

### Spec changes

- `spec/005-StateMachines.md`:
  - §Spawn-spec keys → add `:system-id` row.
  - New §Named addressing via :system-id (cross-linked from §Spawning).
  - §Querying machines → add `(rf/machine-by-system-id sid)`.
  - New §Cross-machine messaging by name.
  - §Capability matrix actor-model axis → add `:actor/system-id` row, claimed by v1 CLJS reference.
- `spec/002-Frames.md` §What lives in a frame → add `[:rf/system-ids]` slot.
- `spec/Spec-Schemas.md` §`:rf.fx/spawn-args` → add `:system-id` to schema.
- `spec/009-Instrumentation.md` §Error contract → add `:rf.error/system-id-collision`.
- `spec/Conventions.md` reserved app-db slots → list `[:rf/system-ids]`.
- `spec/MIGRATION.md` → additive O-N entry (next O-number).

### Implementation (in `implementation/machines/`)

- Extend `spawn-fx` (machines.cljc:962) to:
  1. Update `[:rf/system-ids]` reverse-index when `:system-id` present.
  2. Emit trace event `:rf.machine/system-id-bound` if bound.
  3. Warn-on-rebind if collision: emit `:rf.error/system-id-collision`, last-write-wins for the index update (matches re-frame's existing re-registration semantics).
- Extend `destroy-machine-fx` (machines.cljc:975) to clear the `[:rf/system-ids]` entry and emit `:rf.machine/system-id-released`.
- Add `machine-by-system-id` fn in `re-frame.machines`.
- Late-bind hook `:machines/machine-by-system-id` in `late_bind.cljc`.
- Re-export `re-frame.core/machine-by-system-id` (and optional `dispatch-to-system` sugar) via the late-bind shim.

### IMPORTANT: Discovered precondition (from rf2-ecv4 §2 footnote)

`spawn-fx` and `destroy-machine-fx` (`machines.cljc:962, 975`) currently emit traces but **do not register a handler or initialise a snapshot**. The full lifecycle described in spec 005 §Spawning is partially-realised: id allocation runs, `:on-spawn` writes the id into parent's `:data`, traces fire — but the spawned actor as a *live event handler* isn't actually wired up yet.

**Bundle this work.** Per the rf2-ecv4 finding: "Adding `:system-id` *while* the live-handler wiring is being built is one sweep; later is two." Implement the live-handler wiring as part of this bead so end-to-end testing of `:system-id` resolves to a real running actor.

If the wiring gap is too large to bundle, halt and file a separate bead — but the expectation is bundle.

### Conformance fixtures

- `spawn-with-system-id-then-lookup-resolves` (Spec/conformance pattern — host-agnostic EDN).
- `spawn-without-system-id-leaves-index-empty`.
- `destroy-machine-clears-system-id-index`.
- `system-id-collision-warns-and-rebinds`.

### Tests

- JVM unit: index population, lookup resolution, collision warn, destroy clear.
- CLJS unit: same as JVM (host-agnostic).
- Integration / browser-test: spawn-with-system-id → dispatch-by-system-id reaches the live actor (this exercises the bundled wiring fix).

## Verification

- All artefact test suites green.
- Bundle-isolation grep: examples/counter (no machines used) MUST NOT contain `system-id` strings.
- Production-elision probe: the optional `dispatch-to-system` sugar should DCE-fold cleanly under `:advanced` if unused.

## Sizing

Per rf2-ecv4 §5: 1–2 days for the system-id surface alone. The live-handler wiring bundle adds undetermined scope — the agent reports back if it needs to halt.

## Disposition

Pre-1.0. Direction set; ready to dispatch.
close_reason: Resolved via PR #136 — :system-id named-machine addressing + spawn-fx live-handler wiring shipped (commits 7fc9dfa, bd12c1c, ab92359).

title: Rank XState features missing from re-frame2 and document recommendations
description: User requested a findings document that evaluates XState features not currently present in re-frame2 (excluding tooling), explains what each buys, what existing re-frame2 mechanisms already cover, and ranks them by nice-to-have value. Include a specific assessment of XState systems.

title: Implement multi-artefact CI/CD: topological deploy + lockstep version verification + release-process doc (resolves rf2-w05l)
description:
## Decision (from rf2-w05l)

- Lockstep versions through 1.0 (single root VERSION file).
- Topological-order deploy in release.yml.
- realworld as canonical integration test.
- Reagent canonical substrate; UIx/Helix smoke-tested when landing.
- MIGRATION.md stays flat (M-1..M-NN) through 1.0.

## Implementation tasks

### .github/workflows/release.yml

1. Order deploy jobs: core → schemas → reagent → machines → routing → flows → http → (ssr, epoch when split).
2. Each per-artefact deploy `needs: [previous-artefacts]` so a mid-release failure halts before publishing dependent artefacts to Clojars.
3. Pre-deploy verification job: assert all artefact deps.edn files reference a single VERSION (lockstep contract). Fail if any artefact has drifted.
4. Add a clear failure-recovery comment block at the top: \"If a partial release lands on Clojars, re-tag with vX.Y.Z+1 and re-run; do not attempt to yank — Clojars doesn't support it.\"

### .github/workflows/test.yml

5. Sanity check: verify per-artefact test jobs run in parallel (no false `needs:` dependencies that serialise unnecessarily).
6. Optional: add a 'lockstep version verification' job that runs on every PR (cheap, catches drift before release).

### Documentation

7. Create docs/release-process.md (or extend spec/Conventions.md §Packaging conventions) covering:
   - Tag format: vX.Y.Z (or v0.0.1.beta.N for beta).
   - Trigger: tag push triggers release.yml.
   - Order of deploys (topological DAG diagram).
   - Pre-flight checklist: all tests green on main, VERSION file updated, MIGRATION.md entry added.
   - Recovery: if Clojars publish fails mid-deploy, what to do.
8. Update spec/Conventions.md §Packaging conventions: add 'Lockstep versioning through 1.0' as an explicit row.

### Cross-artefact integration test note

9. Add a comment to examples/realworld/README.md noting it serves as the canonical multi-artefact integration test. This isn't a code change — it's making the canonical-test status legible to future contributors.

### Substrate matrix note

10. Add a note to spec/Conventions.md §Substrate-adapter shipping convention (or wherever substrate-adapter strategy lives): 'Reagent is the canonical substrate; full test suite runs against Reagent. UIx and Helix adapters are smoke-tested only.'

## Verification

- Trigger a dry-run release (push a vX.Y.Z-rc.1 tag to a fork, watch the deploy DAG fire in correct order).
- Verify lockstep version check fails when artefact deps.edn is intentionally desynced (negative test).
- Inspect rendered release-process.md.

## Dependencies

- Wait for rf2-5kpd (HTTP split, in flight) to land — same release.yml file.
- Ideally wait for rf2-uo7v (SSR split) and rf2-lt4e (epoch split) too, so the topological DAG is complete in one pass — but those aren't in flight yet.

## Disposition

Pre-beta blocker. Schedule after rf2-5kpd merges; possibly delay further to bundle with ssr+epoch splits if those are imminent.
close_reason: Resolved via PR #134 — topological deploy DAG, lockstep verification, release-process.md, substrate-matrix all merged (commits 436baf6, f5fa6ec).

title: CI/CD strategy for multi-artefact release coordination
description:
## Context

The Strategy B per-feature artefact split (rf2-5vjj) has produced multiple Maven artefacts that need coordinated CI/CD:
- day8/re-frame-2 (core)
- day8/re-frame-2-schemas (rf2-p7va)
- day8/re-frame-2-machines (rf2-xbtj)
- day8/re-frame-2-routing (rf2-k682)
- day8/re-frame-2-flows (rf2-tfw3)
- day8/re-frame-2-http (rf2-5kpd, in flight)
- ...with ssr (rf2-uo7v), epoch (rf2-lt4e), reagent (already split via rf2-0hxm) still to come

Each per-feature split PR has incrementally added a per-artefact test job to .github/workflows/test.yml and a per-artefact deploy entry to .github/workflows/release.yml. The bigger picture has not been thought through.

## Questions to answer

### Versioning strategy

1. **Lockstep vs independent versions.** Do all artefacts share a single VERSION (current pattern — each per-feature deps.edn reads ../../VERSION), or can each version independently?
   - Lockstep: simpler for users, simpler for changelog, simpler for compatibility matrix. Cost: every artefact ships even when only one changed.
   - Independent: each artefact moves at its own cadence. Cost: compatibility matrix to maintain (re-frame-2-routing 1.4.0 with re-frame-2 1.5.0 — does it work?), bigger user mental model.
2. **Pre-1.0 vs 1.0+ semantics.** Maybe lockstep through the v0.0.1.beta → v1.0 stretch, then independent post-1.0? Or commit to one model now?

### Release coordination

3. **Order of publish.** core → schemas → machines → reagent → routing → flows → http? Some have inter-dependencies (machines depends on core; reagent depends on core). The release workflow needs a topological deploy or it'll break Clojars resolution mid-release.
4. **Atomic-ish releases.** If publish to Clojars succeeds for core but fails for schemas mid-release, what's the rollback story? Clojars doesn't support yank. Options: tag-then-publish (re-tag if a publish fails), publish-from-snapshot-then-promote, or accept that partial-release-recovery is manual.
5. **Beta vs stable channel.** v0.0.1.beta releases are fine to ship loose. Once we're 1.0, the release workflow needs to gate on something stronger — all-tests-green plus reviewer-approval, presumably.

### Testing strategy

6. **Cross-artefact integration tests.** Currently per-artefact tests run via `clojure -M:test` from each artefact's own dir, plus the top-level CLJS browser/elision/examples suites. Where do cross-artefact integration tests live? E.g. \"machines + routing + http used together in a realistic app\" — that's the realworld example today, but is it the canonical integration test or just a demo?
7. **Per-substrate matrix.** When UIx (rf2-3yij) and Helix (rf2-2qit) substrate adapters land, the test matrix multiplies. Do we run the full suite per substrate? Or pick one canonical (Reagent) and smoke-test the others?

### Consumer upgrade story

8. **Migration guide structure.** spec/MIGRATION.md is currently flat (M-1, M-2, ... M-31). For multi-artefact consumers, do we group by artefact (M-routing-1, M-machines-3) or keep flat? Once independent versions land, flat doesn't work.
9. **What does \"upgrade re-frame-2\" mean for a consumer with 4 of our artefacts?** Single bundle update or 4 separate steps?

### Tooling

10. **deps.edn template for new consumers.** A consumer wanting routing + flows + http + schemas + machines reads /implementation/core/deps.edn or just adds them all by hand. Is the README's \"Adding to your project\" section keeping pace with each split?
11. **lein support.** Does any of this work for lein consumers, or is this deps.edn-only?

## Disposition

This is a P2 decision bead — needs input before v0.0.1.beta release. Each split PR has been adding to CI/CD piecemeal; the meta-strategy needs to be set before the release workflow runs in anger.

## Recommendation (placeholder for Mike)

My initial leaning:
- **Lockstep versions through 1.0**, revisit independent for 1.1+. Simpler users-facing story for the early adopter window.
- **Topological-order deploy** with explicit dependency DAG in the release workflow. Hard-code for now, automate later.
- **realworld example as canonical integration test**, augment with a few cross-artefact unit tests as gaps surface.
- **Reagent as the canonical substrate**, smoke-test UIx/Helix when they land.

Open for Mike to redirect.
notes:
---

## Decision (2026-05-09, Mike "agree")

Adopt the recommended disposition:

1. **Lockstep versions through 1.0.** Revisit independent versioning post-1.0. All per-feature artefacts continue reading from the single root VERSION file via `:local/root "../core"`.
2. **Topological-order deploy.** core → schemas → reagent → machines → routing → flows → http (and ssr, epoch when split). Hard-code the DAG in .github/workflows/release.yml using `needs:` linkages.
3. **realworld example as canonical integration test.** Augment with a few cross-artefact unit tests when gaps surface.
4. **Reagent as the canonical substrate.** UIx (rf2-3yij) and Helix (rf2-2qit) adapters smoke-tested when they land; full suite is Reagent.
5. **MIGRATION.md stays flat (M-1..M-NN) through 1.0.** Restructure when independent versioning lands post-1.0.

## Implementation

Filed under separate impl bead — see linked.
close_reason: Resolved via PR #134 — multi-artefact CI/CD strategy implemented (topological deploy DAG, lockstep version verification, release-process doc). Companion bead rf2-ace2 covers the impl side.

title: Bundle: split schemas (Malli) into day8/re-frame-2-schemas (rf2-5vjj feature 1)
description:
First per-feature artefact split per rf2-5vjj Strategy B. Schemas are the heaviest leak (Malli unconditionally bundled in every example) and the most orthogonal — best gain per LOC of work.

## Move

implementation/core/src/re_frame/schemas.cljc -> implementation/schemas/src/re_frame/schemas.cljc
plus tests, plus deps.edn, plus shadow-cljs build target.

## New artefact

day8/re-frame-2-schemas — Malli schemas (Spec 010). Optional dep; users without :rf.schemas/* needs don't include it.

## Core changes

- core.cljc removes any direct require of re-frame.schemas.
- Late-bind any cross-references via the late-bind hook registry (rf2-p8g8 / late_bind.cljc pattern).
- Any tests that exercise schema-aware paths get marked as requiring the schemas artefact.

## Spec edits

- spec/Conventions.md §Packaging — add row for re-frame-2-schemas.
- spec/Ownership.md — schemas surfaces shift to artefact column re-frame-2-schemas.
- spec/MIGRATION.md — M-N entry: existing schema-using apps add the schemas artefact dep.

## Verify

- counter example bundle (no schema usage) — grep for 'malli' / 're-frame.schemas' / 'app-schema' — must be ZERO post-split.
- realworld example bundle (uses schemas) — works as before; bundle includes schemas artefact's strings.
- Bundle-size target: counter raw drops from ~389 KB to ≤380 KB.

## Sequencing

After rf2-NN-packaging-conventions-doc lands so the convention is documented first.

title: Pre-document packaging conventions in spec corpus (rf2-5vjj prerequisite)
description:
Lock in the multi-artefact packaging architecture as normative in the spec corpus, BEFORE any per-feature split lands.

## Edits

### spec/Conventions.md — new §Packaging conventions

Document:
- re-frame2 ships as multiple Maven artefacts.
- Core artefact (day8/re-frame-2): registry, drain, fx, dispatch, subscribe, frame-provider, trace.
- Per-feature artefacts (day8/re-frame-2-<feature-id>): optional capabilities. feature-id matches the spec topic (machines, flows, routing, http, ssr, schemas, epoch).
- Per-substrate artefacts (day8/re-frame-2-<substrate>): rendering substrates (reagent, uix, helix).
- Bundle isolation is structural; each feature is independent (no cross-feature transitive requires from core).

### spec/000-Vision.md — short paragraph under 'What re-frame2 ships'

Cross-links to Conventions §Packaging.

### spec/Ownership.md — new 'Artefact' column

Each surface row gains the artefact that ships it. For features not yet split (everything currently in core except substrate adapters), mark as 'core (split pending: rf2-NN)'.

### README.md Project Layout

Update the directory tree to show the per-artefact structure (currently shows implementation/core, implementation/reagent post-rf2-0hxm).

## Out of scope

- DON'T do any code splits in this bead. Doc-only.
- DON'T touch the README narrative.
- The packaging convention is documented as the norm; per-feature splits land in their own beads.

## Dispatch ordering

Land BEFORE any per-feature split bead. Establishes the rule before code lands.

## Closes / related

- Pre-work for rf2-5vjj.
- Each per-feature split bead inherits this convention as its target.

title: Bundle-size: feature opt-in so unused capabilities don't ship
description:
An app that doesn't use state machines (or flows, or routing, or HTTP-managed, or SSR, or epoch, etc.) shouldn't bundle their code. Currently core probably pulls every feature unconditionally. Two strategies to achieve feature opt-in.

## The two strategies

### Strategy A — Closure DCE within one artefact

Ship one core artefact (day8/re-frame-2) but make every capability lazily / explicitly required.

Current state (probably): core.cljc does (:require [re-frame.machines]), [re-frame.flows], [re-frame.routing], etc. transitively — every feature is on the classpath of every consumer.

Required change: core.cljc requires NONE of the optional features. Each feature is its own ns; the user explicitly requires what they want. With clean cross-references, Closure :advanced DCE strips everything that isn't transitively reachable from user code.

Pros:
- One artefact to maintain, publish, version.
- No breaking change to deps.edn.
- Reuses the elision-probe verification pattern (per-feature: probe for sentinels in advanced build).

Cons:
- Closure DCE has to actually work — any accidental cross-reference (an error message naming a feature ns, a default fallback, a metadata slot mentioning the feature's keyword) prevents elimination.
- Feature ns-load side effects (defonce registrations) must be carefully scoped; if a feature-register-yourself runs unconditionally on ns-load, it's only useful if the ns is required.

### Strategy B — Separate Maven artefacts per feature

Like the substrate-adapter split (rf2-0hxm). Each feature ships as its own artefact:

  day8/re-frame-2          core (registry, drain, fx, basic dispatch/subscribe)
  day8/re-frame-2-machines state machines (Spec 005)
  day8/re-frame-2-flows    flows (Spec 013)
  day8/re-frame-2-routing  routing (Spec 012)
  day8/re-frame-2-http     :rf.http/managed (Spec 014)
  day8/re-frame-2-ssr      SSR (Spec 011)
  day8/re-frame-2-schemas  Malli schemas (Spec 010)
  day8/re-frame-2-epoch    epoch history + restore (Tool-Pair)

User adds the artefact for each feature their app uses.

Pros:
- Bundle isolation guaranteed by structure (Closure doesn't have to be perfect).
- Smaller core for users who want minimum.
- Each feature can version independently if needed.
- Mirrors the substrate-adapter pattern from rf2-0hxm.

Cons:
- Many artefacts to publish in coordination (~7 for v1.0).
- More require lines at app entry.
- Cross-feature interactions become cross-artefact dependency graphs.
- Migration complexity for v1 users — instead of one dep bump, a v1 app picks features.

## Hybrid

Both strategies can coexist:
- **Tier 1 (always-ship in core)**: registry, drain, fx, basic dispatch/subscribe, frame-provider (or basic substrate-coupling), trace (production-elidable).
- **Tier 2 (separate artefacts)**: machines, flows, routing, http, ssr, schemas, epoch.

The split criterion: if a feature is large code, infrequently-used, and self-contained, it's a candidate for separate artefact. If it's small, used everywhere, or deeply intertwined with core, it stays.

## What's the granularity?

Open questions per feature:
- machines (Spec 005) — large, self-contained. Strong candidate for separate artefact.
- flows (Spec 013) — medium, depends on subs. Probably separate.
- routing (Spec 012) — medium, depends on history. Could be either.
- http-managed (Spec 014) — medium, mostly orthogonal. Separate.
- ssr (Spec 011) — JVM-side mostly. Already substrate-shaped.
- schemas (Spec 010) — depends on Malli (heavy dep). Definite separate.
- epoch (Tool-Pair) — Tool-Pair-tier, dev-mode mostly. Production-elidable; could stay or split.
- trace — production-elidable; stays in core (already gated).

## Verification

Per feature:
- Build a production bundle for an app that does NOT use feature X.
- Grep the bundle for X's identifiers (e.g. 'machines', 'reg-machine', 'machine-transition').
- Bundle should be clean — zero hits.

Same pattern as rf2-11hn / rf2-gox8 elision-probe. Generalised.

## Sequencing

This bead needs Mike's strategic call:
1. Strategy A only (clean up cross-references; rely on DCE).
2. Strategy B only (separate artefacts per feature).
3. Hybrid (core tier + separate-artefact tier).

If hybrid, which features land in which tier?

Once direction picked: implementation work is substantial. For Strategy A: ~1 week of cleanup + verification per feature. For Strategy B: ~1-2 days per feature artefact + publishing infrastructure (which rf2-r382 sets up the framework for). For hybrid: combined.

## Cost / value

Bundle size matters most for browser apps targeting size-sensitive contexts (mobile, embedded). For server-side / dev-tooling, less important.

Real-world target: a 'minimum re-frame2 app' (counter-style) should ship a bundle no larger than a re-frame v1 counter app. Currently: probably significantly larger because more feature code is bundled.

Concrete measurement: build the counter example's :advanced bundle today; compare to v1 counter's :advanced bundle. Delta is the cost of the feature drift.

## Cross-references

- Pairs with rf2-0hxm (substrate-adapter split) — same architectural pattern, applied to features instead of substrates.
- Could pair with rf2-r382 (Clojars CD) — multi-artefact publishing infrastructure.
- Decision shape: requires Mike's input on strategy + per-feature placement.

## Out of scope

- The trace / epoch surfaces are already production-elidable per rf2-11hn. They stay in core.
- This bead is the strategy + first-pass implementation. Per-feature splits get filed as their own implementation beads.
- Don't pre-design the perfect hybrid before measuring.
notes:
Strategy decision (Mike, 2026-05-09): Strategy B — separate Maven artefacts per feature. Mirrors the substrate-adapter split (rf2-0hxm) at the feature level.

Measurement still runs (in flight) to establish baseline + confirm which features leak most under current main, but the strategic choice is locked in.

## Per-feature artefacts to ship (post-measurement)

  day8/re-frame-2          core (registry, drain, fx, dispatch, subscribe, frame-provider, trace, basic substrate plumbing)
  day8/re-frame-2-machines state machines (Spec 005)
  day8/re-frame-2-flows    flows (Spec 013)
  day8/re-frame-2-routing  routing (Spec 012)
  day8/re-frame-2-http     :rf.http/managed (Spec 014)
  day8/re-frame-2-ssr      SSR (Spec 011)
  day8/re-frame-2-schemas  Malli schemas (Spec 010)
  day8/re-frame-2-epoch    epoch history + restore (Tool-Pair surfaces)

## Suggested implementation order (smallest/most-isolated first)

1. epoch — already production-elidable; smallest split; least risk.
2. http-managed — orthogonal; recently shipped via Spec 014.
3. ssr — JVM-mostly; substrate-shaped already.
4. schemas — heavy dep (Malli); high-value split for users not using schemas.
5. routing — depends on frame state.
6. flows — depends on sub-cache.
7. machines — biggest; most self-contained code.

Each per-feature split = its own implementation bead, modelled on rf2-0hxm. Cumulative work is significant but each step is bounded.

## Cascading bead updates

- rf2-r382 (Clojars CD): grow scope to publish all 8+ artefacts in coordination.
- rf2-3zee (migration prompt): grow scope to detect feature usage in v1 codebase and add the right per-feature artefact deps.
- spec/MIGRATION.md: per-feature M-N entries describing the dep-list change.
- examples/<name>/deps.edn: each example declares its feature usage explicitly via deps.

## Sequencing

Per-feature splits dispatch one at a time. After measurement lands, file the first per-feature impl bead (epoch most likely as the gentlest first cut).
Documentation requirement (Mike, 2026-05-09): Strategy B's packaging architecture must be documented in the spec corpus as a normative rule.

## Where it lands

1. **spec/Conventions.md — new §Packaging conventions** (canonical home).
   - Multiple Maven artefacts: core + per-feature + per-substrate.
   - Naming convention: day8/re-frame-2-<feature-id> where feature-id matches the spec topic (machines, flows, routing, http, ssr, schemas, epoch).
   - Naming convention for substrates: day8/re-frame-2-<substrate> (reagent, uix, helix).
   - Bundle isolation is structural.
   - Each feature is independent — no cross-feature transitive requires from core.

2. **spec/000-Vision.md** — short paragraph under 'What re-frame2 ships', cross-linking to Conventions §Packaging.

3. **spec/Ownership.md** — gain an 'artefact' column so the surface-to-spec map also tells you which artefact ships it.

4. **README.md Project Layout** — updated as each per-feature split lands.

## Per-feature impl bead template

Each per-feature split bead (rf2-NNNN-feature-<name>-split) must include:
- The artefact split (directory + deps.edn + build target).
- spec/Conventions.md update if first-of-kind (or appended if subsequent).
- spec/Ownership.md row update.
- README.md Project Layout update.
- MIGRATION.md M-N entry for v1 callers.

This documentation rule is normative for v1.0.
## Bundle-size measurements (2026-05-09)

Worktree: re-frame2 origin/main @ 3841ddb. All builds via `shadow-cljs release`. Core artefact (re-frame2/core + reagent adapter) is shared. Bundles are single `main.js` per example.

### Sizes (raw / gzip)

| Example                       | Raw KB | Gz KB | Notes                                  |
|-------------------------------|-------:|------:|----------------------------------------|
| counter                       |  388.9 | 109.3 | minimal — events/subs/views only       |
| temperature (7guis)           |  392.0 |       | minimal                                |
| flight-booker (7guis)         |  394.8 |       | minimal                                |
| timer (7guis)                 |  393.1 |       | minimal                                |
| crud (7guis)                  |  397.3 |       | minimal                                |
| circle-drawer (7guis)         |  397.8 |       | minimal                                |
| cells (7guis)                 |  408.4 |       | minimal                                |
| routing                       |  396.0 |       | uses routing                           |
| todomvc                       |  417.4 | 115.5 | localStorage; minimal otherwise        |
| managed-http-counter          |  393.8 | 110.3 | Spec 014 :rf.http/managed              |
| login                         |  411.5 |       | uses machines                          |
| state-machine-walkthrough     |  409.8 | 116.0 | uses machines                          |
| nine-states                   |  550.4 | 148.3 | uses machines (heavy view code)        |
| ssr                           |  433.5 | 121.1 | uses SSR + hydration                   |
| realworld                     |  513.8 | 140.4 | everything (HTTP, schemas, machines)   |
| **re-frame v1 simple counter**|  **357.4** | **97.0** | **baseline — minimum re-frame app** |

### Feature-leak grid (Y = bundle contains the feature's strings)

| Example                       | mch | flw |  rt | http | ssr | sch |  ep |
|-------------------------------|:---:|:---:|:---:|:----:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| counter                       |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |  Y   |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |
| temperature                   |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |  Y   |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |
| flight-booker                 |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |  Y   |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |
| timer                         |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |  Y   |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |
| nine-states                   |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |  Y   |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |
| routing                       |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |  Y   |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |
| todomvc                       |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |  Y   |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |
| managed-http-counter          |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |  Y   |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |
| crud                          |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |  Y   |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |
| circle-drawer                 |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |  Y   |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |
| cells                         |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |  Y   |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |
| login                         |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |  Y   |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |
| realworld                     |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |  Y   |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |
| state-machine-walkthrough     |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |  Y   |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |
| ssr                           |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |  Y   |  Y  |  Y  |  Y  |
| v1 simple counter (baseline)  |  N  |  Y* |  N  |  N   |  N  |  N  |  N  |

* v1 already bundles `re-frame.flow.alpha` since flows are part of v1 core.

Patterns used (illustrative — concrete strings actually appearing in `:advanced` output, since munged ns names don't survive):

- machines: `rf/machine`, `destroy-machine`, `rf.machine`, `machine.transition`
- flows: `reg-flow-fx!`, `clear-flow-fx!`, `run-flows!`, `rf.fx/reg-flow`, `rf.fx/clear-flow`
- routing: `re-frame.routing`, `rf.routing`, `/navigate`
- http: `rf.http`, `simulate-http`, `http-cancel`, `managed-http`
- ssr: `re-frame.ssr`, `rf.ssr`, `rf/hydrate`, `__rf_payload`
- schemas: `malli`, `frame-schema-entries`, `app-schema`, `schema-validation-failure`
- epoch: `epoch-history`, `reg-epoch`, `rf.epoch`, `epoch-buffer`

Counter bundle's actual quoted strings as proof of leak:
"clear-flow-fx!", "reg-flow-fx!", "run-flows!", "destroy-machine", "rf/machine", "rf/machines", "rf/hydrate", "re-frame.routing", "re-frame.ssr", "rf.ssr", "rf.http", "simulate-http-resolution", "frame-schema-entries", "app-schema", "schema-validation-failure", "malli", and several flow error keywords (:rf.error/flow-bad-inputs, etc.).

### Findings

1. **Every example leaks every feature.** Even minimal apps (counter, the 7guis cluster) bundle code paths from machines, flows, routing, managed HTTP, SSR, schemas, and Malli. The leak is total, not partial.
2. **Cost of feature drift vs v1 baseline.** rf2 counter is 388.9 KB raw / 109.3 KB gzip. v1 simple counter is 357.4 KB raw / 97.0 KB gzip. **Delta: +31.5 KB raw / +12.3 KB gzip** for an app that uses none of the new features.
3. **Heaviest leaks (likely candidates):** Malli pulls a large dependency surface (`!==typeof malli.transform`, etc.) into every bundle — the schemas/Malli leak is probably the single largest size cost. Machines and flows are also dragged in via fx and event handler paths.
4. **Floor for size variation.** Bundles cluster around 388-417 KB unless an app legitimately adds heavy view/SVG/canvas code (nine-states 550, realworld 513). The narrow band confirms core is bundling everything regardless of usage.

### Strategy implications

- **Strategy A (clean DCE within one artefact) requires substantial work.** The cross-references that defeat DCE today are pervasive: error keywords (`:rf.error/flow-*`), keyword-named effects (`:rf.fx/reg-flow`, `:rf.http/managed`), registrar metadata, default-fallback branches, and Malli's heavy unconditional require. Each is a discrete cleanup; 7+ features × multiple touchpoints each. Verifying with the elision-probe pattern is mechanical but laborious, and any regression silently leaks again.
- **Strategy B (separate artefacts) is structurally robust.** If `re-frame.machines` lives in a separate artefact and an app doesn't depend on it, the namespace simply isn't on the classpath — no DCE required. The substrate-adapter split (rf2-0hxm) already proves the pattern works in this repo.
- **Hybrid is the right answer.** Tier 1 (core) ships registry/drain/fx/dispatch/subscribe/frame-provider/trace. Tier 2 (separate artefacts) ships machines, flows, routing, http-managed, ssr, schemas, epoch. This bounds the DCE burden to "core stays small" rather than "every feature must be DCE-perfect", and gives users a concrete bundle dial: each `:deps` line is paid-for capability.

### Recommended starting point

1. **Schemas / Malli first.** It's the heaviest dep and the most clearly orthogonal — schema validation only runs through `frame-schema-entries` registration. Splitting it as `day8/re-frame-2-schemas` removes the Malli surface from every bundle that doesn't opt in. High value, contained scope, mirrors substrate-adapter.
2. **Machines second.** Spec 005 is large, self-contained, and only ~3 of 15 example apps actually use it. Easy to gate.
3. **Defer epoch.** Tool-Pair-tier; already production-elidable per rf2-11hn. Lowest priority.
4. **Apply the elision-probe pattern per artefact** as the verification gate, generalising the rf2-11hn / rf2-gox8 mechanism: probe a feature-N-using app, build for production, grep for the artefact's signature strings; gate-tested in CI.

### Numbers to validate the strategy after first split

Target: rf2 counter bundle ≤ v1 simple counter raw size (357 KB) once schemas + machines + flows + http + routing + ssr + epoch are split out. Realistic intermediate target after Step 1 (schemas/Malli only): ≤ 380 KB raw — recovers the lion's share of the 31.5 KB gap.
close_reason: Resolved via Strategy B per-feature artefact splits — all 7 features shipped (schemas PR #126, machines #129, routing #130, flows #132, http #133, ssr #145, epoch #152) plus the 2 substrate adapters from rf2-0hxm and rf2-3yij. Bundle-isolation CI gate (rf2-51x5) and missing-artefact coverage (rf2-5b6x) ensure regressions cannot recur.

title: Implement source-coord DOM annotation: core capture + Reagent injection + Spec 006 mandate (resolves rf2-z9n1)
description:
Per rf2-z9n1 (a)+(α): ship full source-mapping with mandatory substrate-adapter DOM injection.

## Surfaces

### Core: source-coord capture

reg-view macro emission (already auto-derives id from *ns* + symbol via rf2-d0pi) — extend to capture full source-coord:

  (reg-view sym [args] body)
  ;; emits handler with metadata:
  ;;   :rf/id <ns>/<sym>
  ;;   :rf/source-coord {:ns <ns> :file <file> :line <N> :col <N>}

Source-coord captured via &form metadata + *file* at macroexpansion time. Quoted literals in expansion (per the rf2-d0pi macro-hygiene fix that captured *ns*/*file* at expansion).

Format documented in spec/004-Views.md and spec/Tool-Pair.md; pair-tool consumers (re-frame-pair2) parse as <ns>:<sym>:<line>:<col>.

### Reagent adapter: DOM injection

In implementation/reagent/src/re_frame/substrate/reagent.cljs, wrap reg-view'd component bodies so the outermost hiccup vector receives a :data-rf2-source-coord attribute when interop/debug-enabled? is true.

Impl pattern: hiccup-walk the user's render-fn output; if outermost is a vector starting with a keyword (DOM element), merge the attribute. If outermost is a Reagent component (fn ref) or Fragment (:<>): warn and skip.

Production elision via the standard goog.DEBUG gate — the inject-attribute code path is dead-stripped in :advanced builds.

### Spec 006 mandate

Spec 006 §Substrate adapter contract gains a NEW normative entry:

  Substrate adapters MUST emit :data-rf2-source-coord on rendered DOM root elements when interop/debug-enabled? is true. Components returning React Fragments or non-DOM roots are exempt; tools fall back to :rf/id for those cases. Production builds (goog.DEBUG=false) MUST NOT emit the attribute.

### JVM SSR (Spec 011)

Server-side hiccup→HTML serialiser emits the attribute string-side when in dev mode. Implementation in implementation/core/src/re_frame/ssr.cljc (or wherever hiccup→string lives).

## Tests

- Macro emission: source-coord metadata is correct on registered handlers.
- Reagent adapter: rendered DOM has data-rf2-source-coord attribute on the outermost element.
- Production elision: no source-coord strings in :advanced bundle.
- Fragment exemption: components returning :<> don't get the attribute; warning emitted.
- JVM SSR: server-rendered HTML carries the attribute.

## Spec edits

- spec/004-Views.md: §Source-coord — captured at reg-view, format <ns>:<sym>:<line>:<col>.
- spec/006-ReactiveSubstrate.md §Substrate adapter contract: MUST mandate (with documented Fragment exemption).
- spec/Tool-Pair.md: cross-link from data-rf2-source-coord description to Spec 006 contract.
- spec/011-SSR.md: server-side annotation when in dev mode.
- spec/API.md: rf/source-coord-of (or whatever the lookup fn is named) and the metadata slot.

## Sequencing

Dispatchable now — rf2-0hxm artefact split has merged. Reagent adapter changes go in implementation/reagent/.

UIx (rf2-3yij) and Helix (rf2-2qit) implementations of the same Spec 006 mandate happen in those substrate-specific beads.

## Closes / related

- Resolves rf2-z9n1.
- Cascades scope to rf2-3yij (UIx adapter) and rf2-2qit (Helix adapter): each must implement DOM injection per Spec 006.
close_reason: Resolved via PR #135 — source-coord DOM annotation shipped: capture in core (e29aca3), Reagent injection coverage (130e202), Spec 006 mandate (b6f9dec).

title: Implement re-frame.test-support public surface (resolves rf2-hkr5)
description:
Per rf2-hkr5 Option A: ship public testing helpers in re-frame.test-support.

## Surface to land

  (re-frame.test-support/dispatch-sequence evs)
  (re-frame.test-support/dispatch-sequence evs {:after-each (fn [db ev] ...) :frame :rf/default})
  ;; Fires each event synchronously via dispatch-sync. After each, runs :after-each (or no-op).
  ;; Returns final app-db.

  (re-frame.test-support/assert-state expected-db)
  (re-frame.test-support/assert-state path expected-val)
  ;; Predicate over current app-db. With :frame opt for non-default frames.

  (re-frame.test-support/run-test-sync body)
  ;; Macro wrapping body in test-fixture-style setup. v1 compat shim — body runs under
  ;; the synchronous drain semantics that v2 has by default. Effectively a body-only wrapper;
  ;; primarily for mechanical migration of v1 test code.

## Tests

- dispatch-sequence with simple events; assert final state.
- dispatch-sequence with :after-each that captures intermediate states.
- assert-state at path; mismatch fails clojure.test.
- run-test-sync wraps body; v1 test code that called it ports unchanged.

## Spec edits

- spec/008-Testing.md — describe each helper with signature + example.
- spec/API.md — table rows with the actual ns (re-frame.test-support) and signatures.
- spec/MIGRATION.md — entry for v1 callers: re-frame.test/dispatch-sequence -> re-frame.test-support/dispatch-sequence (rename only). v1 run-test-sync stays (compat shim).

## Implementation notes

- These are thin wrappers over existing internals. ~80 LOC total + tests.
- All three frame-aware via optional :frame opt (defaulting to (current-frame)).
- run-test-sync is a macro for syntactic compat; the others are fns.

## Sequencing

Lands AFTER rf2-0hxm (artefact split) merges — re-frame.test-support might move into a specific artefact. If it stays under core (likely), this bead can land independently. Either way, follow what rf2-0hxm establishes for the test-support placement.

## Closes / related

- Resolves rf2-hkr5.
- Removes dispatch-sequence and assert-state from rf2-gr0n's ambiguous bucket (they become 'add' not 'drop').

title: Implement Option 2 default-adapter resolution for (rf/init!) (resolves rf2-4cb6)
description:
Per rf2-4cb6 Option 2: (rf/init!) with no args picks the registered default substrate adapter; adapters register themselves as default on ns-load.

## Implementation

### Adapter ns side-effect registration

In re-frame.substrate.reagent (and future UIx / Helix adapters), add a defonce that registers the adapter as a default candidate at ns-load time:

  (defonce ^:private __register-default
    (re-frame.substrate.adapter/register-default! adapter))

Or similar — exact API determined by the existing adapter-registration shape in core.

### Core init! resolution

re-frame.core/init!:
- (rf/init! adapter) — explicit adapter object; current behaviour.
- (rf/init!) with no args — looks up the registered default. If exactly one is registered, use it. If multiple, log a warning and pick last-registered (or first-registered — pin in spec). If zero, error with a clear message: 'No substrate adapter registered. Add a substrate dep (e.g. day8/re-frame-2-reagent) and (:require [re-frame.substrate.reagent]).'

### Spec doc

spec/006-ReactiveSubstrate.md — describe the registration contract:
- Adapter ns has a defonce that calls (register-default! adapter) on load.
- (rf/init!) resolves to the registered default.
- Multi-adapter case: pin the policy (last-wins / first-wins / error).

### Tests

- (rf/init!) with no args after requiring re-frame.substrate.reagent → uses Reagent.
- (rf/init!) with no args without any substrate ns required → errors with the message.
- (rf/init! some-adapter) — explicit override still works.
- Idempotency: requiring the substrate ns multiple times doesn't re-register / re-init.

### Examples

Sweep examples to use the no-arg (rf/init!) form. Each example removes the explicit adapter argument from its run fn.

## Sequencing

Lands AFTER rf2-0hxm (artefact split) merges. Until that lands, the adapter ns lives in the same artefact as core, but the registration pattern is conceptually fine.

If rf2-0hxm is still in flight when this bead is dispatched, base on rf2-0hxm-artefact-split branch.

## Closes / related

- Resolves rf2-4cb6.
- Pairs with rf2-0hxm: enables clean (rf/init!) without compromising bundle isolation.
close_reason: Resolved via PR #165 — init! Option 2 default-adapter resolution + multi-adapter error policy + tests + spec docs (commits 0442c7b, 218199b, e6bf305).

title: Add tests for public trace listener contract and delivery semantics
description:
Why this issue exists
The implementation has strong trace-event coverage, but current tests are written almost entirely against the internal bang-form listener functions and per-event delivery. The AI/tool-facing trace surface is load-bearing, and its public API/delivery contract deserves direct tests.

What needs to be done
1. Add tests for the public trace API surface exposed via e-frame.core, not only e-frame.trace/register-trace-cb!.
2. Cover the documented listener arities/options, especially:
- egister-trace-cb with opts
- batched versus per-event delivery semantics
- debounce behavior / batch boundaries
- emove-trace-cb
- 	race-api-version if that surface remains in-spec
3. Assert the ring buffer and live-listener surfaces stay coherent when both are active.
4. If the implementation intentionally diverges from the current spec/API docs, capture that explicitly and tighten tests around the chosen contract.

Current related coverage
- implementation/test/re_frame/trace_test.clj
- implementation/test/re_frame/trace_buffer_test.clj
These cover trace content well, but not the full public listener contract.

title: Schema impl partial — frame-scoping incomplete + app-schemas-digest missing (rf2-xfa2 follow-up)
description:
rf2-xfa2 (PR #104) was supposed to frame-scope app-db schemas. Agent's report claimed: 'Added schemas-by-frame {frame-id {path schema-meta}} mirroring re-frame.flows. reg-app-schema accepts :frame opt; app-schema-at/app-schemas/validate-app-db! are frame-aware'. But Mike (2026-05-09) found:

- spec/010-Schemas.md:181 — schemas described as per-frame.
- spec/Tool-Pair.md:55 — exposes app-schemas-digest for hydration/tool drift detection.
- spec/API.md:220 — app-schemas-digest described.
- impl schemas.cljc:65 — schemas still process-global; explicitly ignores frame scoping.
- impl epoch.cljc:548 — emits :schema-digest-recorded / :schema-digest-current as nil on restore mismatch.

So: spec says X; rf2-xfa2 partially landed X; impl is still partly process-global; app-schemas-digest doesn't exist. The rf2-xfa2 agent shipped less than its report claimed.

## Investigation

1. Read implementation/src/re_frame/schemas.cljc end-to-end. Identify: where does the frame-scoping break? Is the per-frame slot from rf2-xfa2 in place but not consulted? Or is the legacy process-global path still active?

2. Read implementation/src/re_frame/epoch.cljc around line 548. Why are the digest values nil on restore mismatch? Is app-schemas-digest just missing?

3. Read spec/010-Schemas.md, spec/Tool-Pair.md, spec/API.md to confirm what surfaces are advertised.

## Fix

- Complete the frame-scoping migration in schemas.cljc.
- Add app-schemas-digest fn that produces a stable hash over a frame's app-schemas (suitable for cross-run drift detection).
- Wire it into epoch.cljc's restore-schema-mismatch path so the trace carries non-nil :schema-digest-recorded / :schema-digest-current.

## Cross-link

Also relates to rf2-gr0n — app-schemas-digest was on its 'ambiguous' list (spec advertises, impl doesn't ship). This bead lands the impl, removing it from rf2-gr0n's pile.

Discovered by Mike (2026-05-09) as one of three audit findings.

title: API.md ambiguous symbols: per-symbol triage needed (rf2-8hcb follow-up)
description:
rf2-8hcb agent identified 18 advertised public symbols documented in per-spec docs but with NO impl, NO test, NO fixture, and NO example exercising them. Each needs a per-symbol decision: drop the doc, land an impl, or file an EP-shaped bead.

## The 18 items

### Spec 011 SSR
- reg-head — also referenced in docs/guide/07-server-side.md:159
- render-head
- active-head
(:head registry kind exists but no machinery)

### Spec 009 Tracing
- trace-api-version
- with-trace
- merge-trace!
- finish-trace

### Spec 002 Frames lifecycle
- make-restore-fn
- add-post-event-callback
- remove-post-event-callback
- purge-event-queue
- dispatch-and-settle

### Spec 009 Errors
- reg-event-error-handler

### Spec 008 Testing
- dispatch-sequence
- assert-state
(advertised under non-existent re-frame.test ns; namespace is actually re-frame.test-support)

### Spec 011 Platform
- init-platform

### Spec 005 Machines
- spawn-machine
- destroy-machine
(the fx-ids exist; the public fns don't)

## Per-item decisions

For each symbol Mike picks one of:
- (a) Drop from spec — speculative, never used.
- (b) Land impl — needed for v0.0.1.beta.
- (c) Defer — file as own EP-shaped bead, mark as post-v1.

The agent suggested most could go (a) but flagged the SSR-head-related ones as potentially load-bearing for SSR-aware apps.

Discovered from rf2-8hcb.
notes:
Added (rf2-8hcb partial PR #106 follow-up): two more items turned ambiguous on closer look — referenced widely in spec corpus, removing the API.md row would worsen drift.

- **sub-topology** — referenced in 000-Vision, 002-Frames, 006-ReactiveSubstrate, 007-Stories, 008-Testing, 009-Instrumentation, AI-Audit, Construction-Prompts, Principles. Spec describes it as a debugger / introspection surface; impl gap if it ships nowhere.
- **app-schemas-digest** — referenced in 010-Schemas (with code samples) and Tool-Pair.md.

Both need per-item decisions: drop from spec corpus (substantial sweep), land impl, or defer with a clear post-v1 marker.
Resolved (autonomous, 2026-05-09): 11 drops swept; 4 deferred items filed as post-v1 beads (rf2-h96i make-restore-fn, rf2-pgma init-platform, rf2-3i3m ssr-head trio, rf2-8nzo sub-topology). See PR https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/125 for the doc sweep.

title: Guide cross-references to spec/ use repo-relative paths that break on the published site
description:
Many docs/guide/*.md files link to spec docs using '../../spec/*' paths — that's correct for browsing the source tree on GitHub (guide lives at docs/guide/, so '../../' is repo root) but breaks on the published MkDocs site (rf2-h09k) where the staged spec lives at docs/spec/, so the correct relative path is '../spec/*'.

The mkdocs build (rf2-h09k) emits ~30 WARNING entries for these. Build still succeeds; links still work when browsed via GitHub web UI; published site has dead links to spec docs.

## Fix
Sweep docs/guide/*.md and rewrite '../../spec/' -> '../spec/'. Verify both:
- GitHub source-tree browsing still works (it should — '../spec/' from docs/guide/ would resolve to docs/spec/ which doesn't exist in source). 

Hmm — that's the real problem. The two contexts genuinely conflict. Options:
1. Stage docs/guide/ + spec/ -> docs/{guide,spec}/ AND keep links symmetric. Pick one canonical relative form.
2. Run a tiny pre-build link-rewriter (sed) over the staged docs/spec/ tree.
3. Use mkdocs hooks (on_page_markdown) to rewrite '../../spec/' -> '../spec/' at build time.

Option 3 is cleanest — add a tiny on_page_markdown plugin/hook to mkdocs that does the substitution only at build time. Source on disk stays untouched and works for GitHub browsing.

## Acceptance
- 'mkdocs build' produces zero WARNING entries for missing spec link targets.
- GitHub web UI still resolves the same links from docs/guide/.

title: Docs publishing: spec + guide + API to a published site (model on re-frame v1 docs)
description:
Set up published docs for re-frame2 — spec, guide, and human-readable API reference. Model on re-frame v1's docs publishing (MkDocs + Material theme + GitHub Pages, served at day8.github.io/re-frame).

## What to copy from re-frame v1

The day8/re-frame repo has a working docs publishing setup. Look at:
- C:/Users/miket/code/re-frame/mkdocs.yml — MkDocs configuration (theme, navigation, plugins).
- C:/Users/miket/code/re-frame/docs/ — content tree.
- C:/Users/miket/code/re-frame/.github/workflows/ — GHA workflow that builds and publishes (typically push docs/ to gh-pages on tag or push to main).
- C:/Users/miket/code/re-frame/requirements.txt or similar — Python deps for MkDocs build.

Copy the structure across. Adapt for re-frame2's content shape.

## What to publish

Three concerns, one site:

### 1. Spec
Source: spec/*.md (post-rf2-9hft promotion).
Treatment: include in the published site as the formal contract layer. AI-oriented voice OK; flag at top of each page that this is the contract.

### 2. Guide
Source: docs/guide/*.md.
Treatment: human-friendly entry. The published site's primary navigation surface for new users. After the guide, optional drill-down into spec.

### 3. API (human-readable)

The current spec/API.md is a flat consolidated reference table. Convert to per-symbol pages with:
- Symbol name + signature
- Description (one paragraph)
- Code example
- Cross-links to relevant spec sections
- Cross-links to relevant guide chapters

Generate from a single source-of-truth (likely metadata on registrations + docstrings) so the published API stays in sync with the impl.

The auto-generated approach is the v1 model — re-frame.core's docstrings flow into the published API page. Look at how v1 does this (the docs/api/ section under v1's docs tree).

## Deliverables

- mkdocs.yml in the repo root (or wherever v1 puts it).
- Published navigation tree mirroring v1's structure: getting-started / reference / patterns / api / contributing.
- GHA workflow that builds and pushes to gh-pages on push to main.
- A docs/index.md (or README at the published site root).
- API generation pipeline — probably a small Babashka or CLJS script that walks re-frame.core, extracts docstrings + examples, emits Markdown.

## Cross-references

- Pairs with rf2-r382 (Clojars CD) — both are pre-v0.0.1.beta release infrastructure.
- Pairs with rf2-x13a (guide audit + new managed-HTTP chapter) — content has to be ready before publishing.
- Pairs with rf2-au1b (Playwright final-pass) — site shouldn't go up while examples are broken.

## Sequencing

Land:
- After rf2-x13a (guide is current)
- After rf2-9hft (already done — spec is at /spec)
- Before rf2-r382 first publish (so users hitting Clojars find the docs).

## Out of scope

- Don't rewrite content for the published voice; only set up the publishing pipeline.
- Don't pick a different docs-site framework (MkDocs is what re-frame uses; consistency wins).

## Suggested first iteration

1. Copy mkdocs.yml from re-frame v1 verbatim.
2. Adjust nav: spec/ + docs/guide/ + spec/API.md.
3. GHA workflow: build on push to main, push to gh-pages branch.
4. Verify locally: mkdocs serve.
5. First commit lands the structure; auto-published.
6. Iterate on the API generation in a follow-up bead if it's substantial.

title: Please finish or narrow the CLJS source-mapping surface before tooling depends on it
description: The docs currently promise CLJS registration source capture and DOM source annotations that the browser reference host does not consistently ship yet. Please either complete that source-mapping surface or narrow the public contract until it is ready. Tooling and pair-oriented features are already described as depending on this layer, so ambiguity here carries a real AI ergonomics cost.
notes:
Resolved (Mike, 2026-05-09): Option (a) + (α) — ship full source-mapping AND make DOM annotation MANDATORY in Spec 006's substrate adapter contract.

## What ships

- **Core**: macro-side source-coord capture at reg-view expansion (already partial via rf2-d0pi auto-id-from-*ns*; this completes the surface to capture file/line/col/ns).
- **Reagent adapter**: hiccup-walk wraps outermost vector with :data-rf2-source-coord attribute when goog.DEBUG is true.
- **Spec 006 mandate**: substrate adapters MUST inject :data-rf2-source-coord on rendered DOM root when goog.DEBUG is true. Documented exception: components returning React Fragments or non-DOM roots are exempt; pair tools fall back to :rf/id for those.
- **JVM SSR (Spec 011)**: server-side equivalent — emit data-rf2-source-coord on the rendered HTML when in dev mode.

## Bead-scope cascading

- rf2-3yij (UIx adapter): scope grows to include DOM injection per Spec 006 mandate. Either macro-AST-rewrite (Option 2a) or React.cloneElement wrap (Option 2b). Implementation bead tracks this as part of the adapter work.
- rf2-2qit (Helix adapter): same.

## Production elision

All DOM annotation is gated on interop/debug-enabled? (goog.DEBUG mirror) — production bundles are clean of source-coord strings. Verified via existing elision-probe pattern (rf2-11hn / rf2-gox8).

Implementation bead filed.
close_reason: Resolved via PR #135 — source-coord direction-set + impl merged together with rf2-z7f7.

title: Please move routing ephemeral state back under the frame boundary
description: Review feedback found process-global routing ephemera such as scroll-restoration state and navigation-token allocation living outside the frame boundary. Please move that state under the frame model, or explicitly narrow the contract to one routed frame per process. Keeping these values global makes multi-frame, story, replay, and SSR behavior harder to keep deterministic.

title: Please harden frame teardown against post-drain background errors
description: A local JVM test run completed green but still emitted a background NullPointerException in the router or adapter path after teardown. Please guard pending drains and adapter writes so destroying frames or disposing runtime state cannot leave late async work trying to write through a nil container. This would make the test surface cleaner and protect frame lifecycle guarantees under stress.
notes:
Halted (agent watchdog timeout, 2026-05-09). Agent began designing per-frame timer registry + late-bind hooks; uncommitted late_bind.cljc work discarded. Mike to redirect direction in morning — work may be over-scoped vs the bead's narrow 'harden teardown error paths' intent.
Concrete reproducer (Mike, 2026-05-09): scheduled drain races frame destruction, then writes through nil container.

Path:
- router.cljc:255 — scheduled drain.
- frame.cljc:181 — frame destruction.
- router.cljc:179 — unconditional replace-container! call.
- substrate/plain_atom.cljc:15 — nil-assuming adapter.

Targeted fix: guard replace-container! against nil container. drain-on-destroyed-frame becomes a documented no-op (or :rf.warning trace). Single load-bearing change, ~10 LOC + tests. Ignore the earlier 'redesign teardown' framing — Mike wants the narrow null-safety fix.

title: Please refresh stale spec and status references across the corpus
description: The review found several stale or contradictory references, including old goal numbers, outdated implementation status counts, and a phantom Spec 003 row in the implementation status table. Please run a focused drift sweep across the corpus and replace brittle hard-coded counts or outdated cross-references with current wording. This is small mechanically, but it meaningfully improves trust in a spec-first repository.

title: Please auto-select the Reagent adapter for CLJS browser boots
description: Review feedback suggests the default CLJS boot path still installs the plain-atom adapter unless callers know to override it manually, even though the spec positions Reagent as the browser default. Please make the default adapter selection follow the documented target-based contract. That would reduce hidden setup knowledge and keep the reference runtime aligned with its own substrate story.
notes:
Resolved (Mike, 2026-05-09): Option 2 — adapter ns registers itself as default on load via defonce. (rf/init!) with no args picks the registered default.

User pattern:

  (:require [re-frame.core :as rf]
            [re-frame.substrate.reagent])  ; ns-load auto-registers as default
  (rf/init!)                                 ; uses Reagent automatically

Multi-substrate apps still pass an explicit adapter to (rf/init! some-adapter). Default-resolution is last-wins (or first-registered — pin in spec).

Compatible with rf2-0hxm artefact split: the registration is a side-effect of the user requiring the adapter ns; core never references the adapter ns directly.

Implementation bead filed; lands after rf2-0hxm merges.
close_reason: Resolved via PR #165 — init! Option 2 direction confirmed and impl shipped via rf2-84po (commit 0442c7b).

title: Please stabilize the browser test harness and isolate example mount side effects
description: The browser harness currently appears to rely on weak server ownership assumptions, and review feedback also found example-specific side effects leaking into browser verification. Please make the harness positively identify the server and assets it owns, then separate headless example logic from real DOM mount behavior where needed. This would turn the browser suite into a deterministic signal instead of a partly incidental one.
notes: Halted twice (agent watchdog timeouts, 2026-05-09). Both attempts modified examples/nine_states/core.cljs + examples/realworld/core.cljs (probably namespacing event-ids further) but timed out before committing. Browser test runs (npm run test:browser, 3x for stability verification) suspected as the long-running step that triggers the watchdog. Mike to redirect or take a different approach in the morning.

title: Please pin the cross-spec interaction rules with executable conformance fixtures
description: The repo documents twenty cross-spec interaction rules, but they remain provisional and several related CLJS tests are placeholders or shape-only checks. Please promote the highest-risk interactions into host-agnostic conformance fixtures so those decisions stop living only in prose plus reference-host knowledge. That would materially strengthen the AI-first claim that the corpus, not the existing codebase, is the authoritative executable contract.

title: Please integrate Spec 014 into the canonical corpus map and support docs
description: Spec 014 now exists as a shipped optional capability with fixtures and implementation support, but the canonical spec index, reading paths, conventions, and conformance capability taxonomy still do not treat it as part of the discoverable corpus. Please thread Spec 014 through the top-level maps and add the missing API or schema support references around the rf.http surface. That would make the corpus internally closed around a capability it already claims and tests.
notes: Closed (autonomous, 2026-05-09): PR #111 landed the corpus integration. spec/API.md, spec/Conventions.md, spec/Ownership.md, spec/README.md updated with Spec 014 cross-references.

title: Please ship or explicitly de-scope the documented re-frame.test surface
description: Spec 008 and API.md currently promise a public re-frame.test namespace plus helpers such as dispatch-sequence, assert-state, and run-test-sync compatibility, but the implementation only exposes internal test-support helpers. Please either land the promised public testing surface or remove the claim from contract-bearing docs. This would make the testing story self-consistent for users and porting agents.
notes:
Resolved (Mike, 2026-05-09): Option A — ship the public testing surface.

Land in re-frame.test-support:
- (dispatch-sequence [evs] {:after-each (fn [db ev] ...)?}) — fires each event synchronously; runs optional :after-each predicate. Returns final app-db.
- (assert-state expected-db) or (assert-state [path expected-val]) — predicate over current app-db (frame-aware via :frame opt).
- (run-test-sync body) — v1 compat shim. v2 drain is already synchronous via dispatch-sync, so the macro becomes a body-only wrapper. Migration agent leaves v1 calls in place.

Spec 008 + API.md describe the actual signatures. MIGRATION.md gains a row pointing v1 callers at the canonical v2 names.

Implementation bead filed.

title: Please close the effect-map contract drift in the migration and guide docs
description: The locked effect-map contract is now a closed {:db ... :fx [...]} shape, but some migration and guide material still talks as if the v1 top-level effect keys remain the outer contract. Please rewrite those sections so they preserve the re-frame feel without obscuring the actual simplification. That would remove a direct mental-model mismatch at one of the first entry points for upgraders.
notes:
Closed (autonomous, 2026-05-09): rf2-3wme audit agent found zero drift. Per-file sweep across docs/guide/, spec/MIGRATION.md, spec/API.md, spec/Conventions.md, spec/002-Frames.md, all Pattern-* docs, and Construction-Prompts.md — every :dispatch / :dispatch-later mention is correctly contextualised: either (a) explicitly labeled v1 prose, (b) fx-id inside the canonical :fx [[fx-id args]] vector, or (c) fx-id key in a :fx-overrides registry. M-8 contract is upheld throughout v2 prose. Likely already-resolved by rf2-7cco audit (PR #85), rf2-x13a guide audit (PR #92), rf2-ju9o link sweep (PR #100), or rf2-vuj5 ch.04 alignment (PR #97).

If a specific passage shows v2-using-v1-shape drift later, file a new bead with the exact location.

title: CD: publish to Clojars (per-artefact); model on re-frame v1's release workflow
description:
Set up Clojars publishing for re-frame2 and associated substrate adapters. Anticipate v0.0.1.beta as the first release.

## What to copy from re-frame v1

The day8/re-frame repo has a working release workflow. Look at:
- C:/Users/miket/code/re-frame/.github/workflows/ — specifically the release / publish workflows.
- C:/Users/miket/code/re-frame/deps.edn — the release alias / build target config.
- C:/Users/miket/code/re-frame/release.sh or similar release-orchestration script (if present).
- C:/Users/miket/code/re-frame/CHANGELOG.md — release-cadence convention.

Copy the structure across. Adapt for re-frame2's specifics.

## Artefacts to publish

Per rf2-0hxm (artefact split), each substrate ships separately:
- day8/re-frame-2 — core (registry, drain, machines, flows, routing, fx, schemas, trace)
- day8/re-frame-2-reagent — Reagent adapter
- day8/re-frame-2-uix — UIx adapter (when rf2-3yij ships; possibly later)
- day8/re-frame-2-helix — Helix adapter (when rf2-2qit ships; later)

Initial release ships:
- day8/re-frame-2 v0.0.1.beta
- day8/re-frame-2-reagent v0.0.1.beta

UIx and Helix follow when their adapter beads land.

## CD pipeline shape

Suggested workflow:
1. Tag the repo with the release version (e.g. v0.0.1.beta).
2. GHA workflow triggered by the tag.
3. For each artefact:
   - Run JVM + CLJS test suites (gate on green).
   - Run elision probes.
   - Run Playwright examples.
   - Build the artefact (jar / pom).
   - Push to Clojars via deps-deploy or similar.
4. Generate release notes from CHANGELOG / commit history.
5. Create a GitHub release with the tag + notes.

## Things to set up

- Clojars credentials in GHA secrets (CLOJARS_USERNAME, CLOJARS_PASSWORD).
- Per-artefact deps.edn / pom.xml / build.clj configuration.
- A release.edn or similar config naming the artefacts and their published coordinates.
- CHANGELOG.md (if not already present).

## Sequencing

This bead's full implementation depends on rf2-0hxm (artefact split into separate Maven coords). If rf2-0hxm isn't done yet, scope this bead to:
1. Set up CI/CD for the SINGLE current artefact (whatever that ships as today).
2. Document the multi-artefact extension as a follow-up.

Or wait for rf2-0hxm to land and do both at once.

## Out of scope

- Don't actually publish v0.0.1.beta in this bead. Just set up the pipeline. Mike will do the first publish manually to confirm credentials work, then subsequent releases automate.
- Don't redesign release cadence or CHANGELOG format from re-frame v1's conventions. Copy them.

## Closes / related

- Pairs with rf2-0hxm (artefact split — multi-artefact publishing depends on it).
- Pairs with rf2-3zee (migration prompt now points at day8/re-frame-2 — Clojars must actually have it).
- Triggered by Mike's intent to ship v0.0.1.beta soon.
notes:
2026-05-09 bookkeeping: PR #121 shipped per-artefact Clojars CD (release.yml). Final scope decision (which artefacts ship in v0.0.1.beta channel, beta/stable gating, atomic-rollback policy if mid-release Clojars publish fails) still pending Mike's call. Leaving in_progress until Mike answers those open questions.

---

## Re-scope (Mike, 2026-05-09)

Impl side shipped via PR #121 (earlier session). The "missing-by-design" framing in the rf2-o423 audit is now stale — Clojars CD is wired up.

**Remaining scope** (this bead refocused to policy-only):
- Beta vs stable channel gating policy.
- Atomic-rollback policy when a multi-artefact deploy partially succeeds (Clojars doesn't yank).
- Whether v0.0.1.beta releases should be hand-cut or pipeline-driven.

The lockstep-version verifier and topological deploy DAG (rf2-ace2 / PR #134) are in place; this bead now tracks the operational policy decisions, not the missing-impl gap.

title: [bug] nine-states-runs-end-to-end browser test failing on main
description:
rf2-9hft (PR #87) agent reported the nine-states-runs-end-to-end browser test fails on main and verified the failure was pre-existing (not introduced by the spec rename).

The test at implementation/test/.../runtime_cljs_test.cljs (or similar) must have started failing after some recent merge. Suspects: rf2-piag (still in PR #84 — not merged), rf2-een2, rf2-jp75, rf2-25aq Reagent v2 — any of these could have introduced subtle browser-render differences for the nine-states machine demo.

## Investigation steps

1. Run npm run test:browser on origin/main; confirm the failure shape and message.
2. git bisect between recent merges to identify the introducing commit.
3. Determine fix direction:
   - If the test was wrong (assertion against a stale shape), update.
   - If the impl was wrong (regression), fix the impl.
   - If the example was wrong (re-frame2 idiom drift), fix the example.

## Out of scope

- This bead is for the test failure only. If the bisect reveals a deeper machine-substrate regression, file follow-up beads.
- Don't fix forward by skipping/deleting the test. Make it pass.

Discovered from rf2-9hft PR #87 testing.

title: examples/ audit: verify all examples follow current best practice idioms
description:
Sweep every example under examples/ to verify it uses current re-frame2 idioms — surface drift before users see stale patterns.

## Idiom checklist (sweep each example against this)

- **reg-view defn-shape** (rf2-d0pi). No (reg-view :keyword (fn [] ...)) calls; all defn-shape (reg-view sym [args] body). Auto-injected dispatch / subscribe used inside view bodies (no manual capture unless legitimately needed).
- **view (not get-view)** (rf2-sx7t / rf2-heo2). All runtime view lookups use (rf/view :id), not (rf/get-view :id).
- **Form 2 canonical** (rf2-iyzm). Render trees use [my-view args], not [:my-view args]. (Keyword vectors at render time are HTML elements per spec.)
- **rf/frame-provider canonical** (rf2-41la / rf2-sixo). No direct calls to build-frame-provider; user code uses [rf/frame-provider {:frame ...} ...].
- **No h macro** (rf2-n4um / rf2-u33b). No (rf/h ...) wrapper calls.
- **Machine bodies — data-direct contract** (rf2-qppo). guards/actions/sub-machine sub fns use (fn [data event] ...), not (fn [{:keys [data]} _] ...). Optional 3-arity (fn [data event ctx] ...) for snapshot-introspection.
- **:on-spawn paths data-relative** (rf2-smba / rf2-een2). :set [:foo] not :set [:data :foo].
- **Spec 014 for HTTP** (rf2-z1mw / rf2-cfig). Any HTTP requests use :rf.http/managed (or canned-success / canned-failure stubs in tests). No ad-hoc (js/fetch ...) calls in user code.
- **M-8 effect-map shape** (rf2-ooc5). {:db ... :fx [...]}; no top-level :dispatch in event returns. (The policer enforces; check examples don't fight it.)
- **No re-frame.alpha** (rf2-7cb2). No (reg :event-fx ...), no (sub {...}), no :re-frame/lifecycle annotations.
- **No re-frame-10x dependency** (matches re-frame-pair2 constraint). Examples should use Tool-Pair surfaces (epoch-history etc.) for introspection, not 10x.
- **:interceptors positional, not in metadata-map** (rf2-bbea). Interceptors as the third positional arg, not inside the metadata-map.
- **dispatch-sync only when synchronous commit is required** (rf2-u95c lesson). E.g., Reset operations that need DOM commit before next tick.
- **No :context (use :data)** for state machine snapshots (rf2-hbt1).

## Examples to audit



Plus examples/playwright/*.spec.cjs — Playwright specs should also use current idioms (e.g. test data-test-id attributes the example exposes).

## Process

1. For each example, grep for anti-patterns (old reg-view shape, get-view, h macro, etc.).
2. Read each example's source end-to-end. Note any drift not caught by grep.
3. Fix in place. Each example fix is small enough to land in one commit per example, OR group by anti-pattern across examples (e.g. one commit per idiom swept).
4. Verify all suites: clojure -M:test, test:cljs, test:browser, test:elision, test:examples.

## Out of scope

- Don't add new examples or new tests.
- Don't refactor for ergonomics — only sweep idiom drift. If an example is structurally fine but stylistically dated, leave alone.
- Don't touch implementation/ unless an example reveals a real impl bug — file a follow-up bead instead.

## Sequencing

Land AFTER:
- rf2-9hft (spec promotion to /spec) — examples don't move but doc cross-references might.
- rf2-4v73 (realworld test harness extraction) — touches realworld; sweep after the relocation.
- PR #84 (rf2-piag :render-key tuple) — examples may grow assertions about :render-key.

This bead's PR adjusts after those land.

## Closes / related

- Sibling to rf2-x13a (guide audit) but for examples.
- Surfaces drift the rf2-7cco spec audit identified at the spec layer; this is the same audit at the example layer.

title: Multi-axis repo review and bead filing
description: Review the current re-frame2 repo against its stated goals and specification from five angles: completeness, correctness, clarity/simplicity, best practice, and testing. Consolidate actionable observations, dedupe against existing beads, and create follow-up beads for uncovered gaps.
close_reason: Completed the multi-axis review and filed follow-up suggestion beads for the actionable findings.

title: Substrate adapters ship as separate artefacts (day8/re-frame-2-{reagent,uix,helix})
description:
Bundle isolation: a Reagent-only app must not transitively pull in UIx or Helix code, and vice versa. Cleanest path is separate artefacts per substrate.

## The proposal

  day8/re-frame-2          core: registry, drain, machines, flows, routing, fx, schemas, trace
  day8/re-frame-2-reagent  Reagent adapter only
  day8/re-frame-2-uix      UIx adapter only (when rf2-3yij ships)
  day8/re-frame-2-helix    Helix adapter only (when rf2-2qit ships)

User picks substrate by adding the relevant adapter artefact:

  ;; Reagent app
  re-frame/re-frame-2          {:mvn/version "..."}
  re-frame/re-frame-2-reagent  {:mvn/version "..."}

  ;; UIx app
  re-frame/re-frame-2     {:mvn/version "..."}
  re-frame/re-frame-2-uix {:mvn/version "..."}

The substrate adapter ns lives in its own artefact:
  re-frame.substrate.reagent  -> in re-frame-2-reagent only
  re-frame.substrate.uix      -> in re-frame-2-uix only
  re-frame.substrate.helix    -> in re-frame-2-helix only

## Why

- Bundle isolation guaranteed by structure, not by careful coding.
- Closure DCE doesn't have to be perfect — a Reagent-only user simply doesn't have UIx code on the classpath.
- Reinforces 'substrate is independent of core' thesis structurally.
- Same pattern as the wider Clojure ecosystem (reagent is one artefact; re-frame is another).

## Adjustments to existing beads

- rf2-5sqd (artefact name) — now: day8/re-frame-2 is the CORE artefact; substrate adapters are siblings. The Option-2 decision still holds; just add the adapter siblings.
- rf2-3yij (UIx adapter) — implement in a NEW artefact day8/re-frame-2-uix, not as a namespace inside the core artefact.
- rf2-2qit (Helix adapter) — same, separate artefact.
- rf2-3zee (migration prompt) — when bumping deps, add the right adapter artefact alongside the core. Detect existing reagent dep -> add re-frame-2-reagent. Detect uix dep -> add re-frame-2-uix. Etc.

## Verification

After each substrate adapter ships:
- Build a production bundle for an app that uses only that substrate.
- Grep the bundle for the other substrates' library names ('reagent.core', 'uix', 'helix').
- Bundle should be clean — zero hits.

This is similar in spirit to the elision-probe (rf2-11hn / rf2-gox8) but cross-substrate rather than cross-debug-mode.

## Sequencing

1. Land this artefact-split decision (this bead) BEFORE rf2-3yij or rf2-2qit dispatch their implementations — both depend on knowing the artefact layout.
2. Move the existing re-frame.substrate.reagent ns into a new artefact directory (e.g. implementation-reagent/ alongside implementation/).
3. Adjust the build / publishing config to produce 2 artefacts initially (re-frame-2 and re-frame-2-reagent).
4. Each subsequent substrate gets its own artefact under the same pattern.

## Out of scope

- Don't drop Reagent support to migrate.
- Don't unify re-frame.core's substrate-config plumbing yet — keep substrate-aware reg-view paths as currently planned in rf2-3yij.

## Trigger

This is meta-infrastructure that pairs with rf2-3yij. Schedule together: this bead first (sets up the artefact split), then rf2-3yij (UIx adapter goes into its own artefact).

title: Substrate adapter: UIx
description:
Add UIx as a Spec 006 substrate adapter, parallel to the existing Reagent adapter. UIx is the recommended modern hooks-based CLJS-React layer; this bead makes re-frame2 work end-to-end under it.

## Scope

### Adapter at re-frame.substrate.uix

Implement the Spec 006 adapter contract for UIx:
- Subscription resolution — use-subscribe hook wrapping useSyncExternalStore. Subscribes on mount, returns current value, cleans up on unmount.
- Component-local-state primitives — useState / useRef instead of r/atom / r/with-let.
