- frame teardown vs scheduled drain race causing post-test NullPointerException
- trace contract drift between spec/API and implementation
- schema tooling surface drift, especially per-frame schemas and pp-schemas-digest
2. Evaluate a narrow formal-verification pilot around the pure seams:
- machine-transition
- compute-sub
- epoch replay / restore invariants
- routing / managed-http stale-result and retry invariants
3. Evaluate a runtime-monitoring path over trace / epoch output:
- define monitorable invariants over trace events and epoch records
- decide whether this should be an internal harness, a pair-tool feature, or a separate tool
4. Decide whether the long-term shape is:
- verified kernels with an unverified shell
- monitor-only enforcement over logs / traces
- or a hybrid approach
Notes from the review
- JVM tests passed (223 tests, 1047 assertions, 78/78 conformance fixtures) but a background drain thread still threw a NullPointerException after completion, indicating a frame-lifecycle / async-drain race worth fixing before replay/verification work builds on it.
- The repo is unusually well-positioned for this line of work because the spec is already first-class and much of the state evolution surface is data-driven and host-portable.
External sources to use when revisiting
- lemmafit: https://midspiral.com/blog/introducing-lemmafit-a-verifier-in-the-ai-loop/
- Building a React App with Formally Verified State: https://midspiral.com/blog/building-a-react-app-with-formally-verified-state/
- dafny-replay: https://github.com/metareflection/dafny-replay
- PObserve runtime monitoring: https://p-org.github.io/P/advanced/pobserve/pobserve/
Likely inspiration to assess
- external deterministic verifier loop for AI-generated logic
- verified state kernels with compiled/runtime integration
- replay / undo / authority / effect-orchestration kernels
- runtime monitoring over logs/traces against formalized invariants

title: Trace contract spec lines still stale (rf2-j7kv follow-up)
description:
rf2-j7kv (PR #107) reconciled the trace contract — but Mike found these specific lines still describe the unshipped span/batched/3-arity surface:

- spec/009-Instrumentation.md:21 — describes span-shape events, batched/debounced delivery, (register-trace-cb key cb opts)
- spec/API.md:247 — same

Action: read those lines and any neighbouring stale prose; align with the synchronous, point-event, 2-arity (register-trace-cb! key cb) surface that ships.

Likely root cause: rf2-j7kv's sweep was less thorough than the agent's report suggested. Now we know which lines specifically — focused fix.

Discovered by Mike (2026-05-09) as one of three audit findings.

title: Cross-Spec doc §6 — reconcile :rf.warning/nav-fx-on-server vs :rf.fx/skipped-on-platform trace
description: Cross-Spec-Interactions.md §6 (Routing in SSR) says 'Navigation effects (:rf.nav/push-url, :rf.nav/replace-url) are no-ops on the server with a :rf.warning/nav-fx-on-server trace.' The runtime actually emits :rf.fx/skipped-on-platform (the generic platform-gate trace from re-frame.fx/handle-one-fx) — there is no nav-specific :rf.warning/nav-fx-on-server trace. Surfaced from rf2-bhhu while pinning §6 — the new fixture asserts what the runtime does. Either the prose should be updated to cite :rf.fx/skipped-on-platform (the generic trace is sufficient — the :fx-id tag distinguishes nav from other platform-gated fx) or a dedicated :rf.warning/nav-fx-on-server trace should be added. Recommendation: prose update; the generic trace is enough.

title: Cross-Spec doc §7 — reconcile route-not-found / error-projector contradiction
description: Cross-Spec-Interactions.md §7 (Route-not-found under SSR) says 'The error projector does NOT fire — route-not-found is normal control flow, not an error.' But the runtime (re-frame.routing/handle-url-change) explicitly emits :rf.error/no-such-handler, and re-frame.ssr/default-error-projector-fn maps that error category to {:status 404 :code :not-found ...} — the :ssr/error-known-mapping fixture pins exactly that behaviour. The new cross-spec-route-not-found-ssr-status.edn fixture (filed under rf2-bhhu) reflects the runtime, not the prose. Either the prose is wrong (and should be updated to say 'the projector DOES fire — its known-mapping turns route-not-found into the locked 404 public-error') or the runtime is wrong (and route-not-found should bypass the error projector with a dedicated 404 path). Recommendation: prose is wrong — the projector firing IS the wire-level signal that produces the 404; bypassing it would mean every host re-implements the not-found-→-404 mapping. Update §7 accordingly.

title: Machines :after must no-op under :ssr-server / :platform :server
description: Cross-Spec interaction #4 (Machines × SSR) specifies that :after is a no-op under :ssr-server: 'The entry action skips timer scheduling; the synthetic timer-elapsed event is never queued; the request frame is destroyed before any timer could fire anyway.' Today re-frame.machines/transition-with-actions schedules :after timers unconditionally — there is no platform-aware gating in machines.cljc. Surfaced from rf2-bhhu (cross-spec fixtures) — the rule is documented but the runtime doesn't yet enforce it. Resolution: thread the active frame's :platform into create-machine-handler (or read it from :rf/frame on the machine spec the handler stamps), skip the :rf.machine.timer/scheduled emission and the timer-arming branch when :platform = :server, and emit :rf.machine.timer/skipped-on-server (or similar) trace for visibility. Then author the corresponding cross-spec fixture (cross-spec-after-no-op-under-ssr.edn) and flip §4 to Pinned.

title: Conformance runner: realise reg-machine bodies for cross-spec error fixtures
description: Cross-Spec interactions #11 (machine action throws), #12 (effect handler throws inside machine action's :fx) and #17 (machine error inside SSR) cannot be pinned by conformance fixtures today because the runner only supports direct :machine-transition calls. The runtime's :rf.error/machine-action-exception handling lives inside re-frame.machines/create-machine-handler — which is only reached when the machine is registered via reg-machine and dispatched as an event. Surfaced from rf2-bhhu (cross-spec fixtures). Resolution: extend conformance_test.clj's realise-handlers to call rf/reg-machine for :fixture/registry :machine entries (with bodies in :fixture/handlers :machine), and add the missing :throw op to the machine-action body interpreter. Then author cross-spec-machine-action-throws.edn / cross-spec-machine-fx-handler-throws.edn / cross-spec-ssr-machine-error.edn and flip Cross-Spec-Interactions §11/12/17 to Pinned.

title: Add Playwright specs and shadow-cljs builds for ssr and state-machine-walkthrough examples
description: rf2-au1b found these two examples are listed in examples/README.md (ssr as a Pedagogical sketch) but have no Playwright spec and no shadow-cljs build target in implementation/shadow-cljs.edn. The examples currently exist only as core.cljc reference sketches. To bring them under the same end-to-end verification umbrella as every other example: (1) add an index.html host page for each, (2) add a shadow-cljs build target to implementation/shadow-cljs.edn, (3) wire them into the EXAMPLES list in implementation/scripts/serve-and-run-examples-tests.cjs, (4) author a <name>.spec.cjs that asserts initial render plus one interaction per the rf2-au1b acceptance bar. Discovered while running rf2-au1b's verification pass.

title: MkDocs build: stage examples/ + root README.md, or rewrite cross-tree refs (rf2-qvlf follow-up)
description:
rf2-qvlf agent surfaced 33 remaining build warnings after the guide-spec rewrite. The remaining patterns reference ../../examples/* and ../../README.md from inside guide/ and spec/ source files. The mkdocs build doesn't stage examples/ or the root README into docs/, so those links 404 on the published site.

## Two paths

1. **Stage more.** Update .github/workflows/docs.yml to stage examples/* and root README.md alongside the existing spec/ staging step. Adjust mkdocs.yml nav if examples should appear in the site.

2. **Rewrite to GitHub URLs.** Add another rewrite case in mkdocs_hooks.py: ../../examples/X -> https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/blob/main/examples/X (and similarly for README). Source stays GitHub-friendly; published site links go to the GitHub source-tree view.

Path 2 is simpler and probably right — the examples are CLJS apps, not docs to render. Embedding them in the site as docs would require a different treatment (probably extract their READMEs only, not the full source).

Path 1 is right if examples have READMEs we want users to read on the published site as a 'gallery of worked examples' page. That's a more substantial design move.

Recommend Path 2 unless there's a desire for a published examples gallery.

Discovered from rf2-qvlf (PR #98).

title: Frame preset expansion lags Spec 002 §Frame presets
Guide ch.02 minimal-mount narrative — add init! / dispatch-sync callout
description:
Chapter 02 mount example elides (rf/init! reagent-adapter/adapter) and (rf/dispatch-sync [:counter/initialise]) for narrative simplicity. The runnable counter example at examples/counter/core.cljs does both.

Cleanup: either add a brief 'minimum-viable mount' callout to the chapter, or extract a 'minimal mount block' pattern doc. Either keeps the narrative simple while pointing newcomers at the runnable shape.

Probably fine to land as a tiny doc tweak in chapter 02. Or fold into rf2-zuwz (examples best-practice audit) if it surfaces as a pattern.

Discovered from rf2-x13a guide audit.

title: Guide ch.08 references :platforms #{:server} fx-gating; verify impl coverage
description:
Chapter 08 (v1 migration) §'Three v1 surfaces removed' lists :platforms #{:server} and :rf.fx/skipped-on-platform as part of the new surface.

The rf2-x13a guide-audit agent didn't find these in the impl test surface they checked. Could be:
- Already implemented in a sibling impl ns the agent didn't sweep.
- A forward-promise the guide is making.
- Missing impl that needs to land before v0.0.1.beta.

Investigation: grep -rn ':platforms.*:server\|:rf\.fx/skipped-on-platform' implementation/. If empty, file an impl bead. If present, the guide is correct.

Discovered from rf2-x13a guide audit.
notes:
Verified impl coverage. Both surfaces are present and exercised:

- :platforms #{:server} appears throughout SSR fxs in implementation/src/re_frame/ssr.cljc (lines 444, 464, 474, 484, 494, 512). :platforms metadata generally is plumbed via implementation/src/re_frame/fx.cljc :48 (active platform set on each fx).
- :rf.fx/skipped-on-platform fires from implementation/src/re_frame/fx.cljc :190, implementation/src/re_frame/routing.cljc :895/903/928. The trace event is asserted in implementation/test/re_frame/trace_test.clj :155, 305-308; implementation/test/re_frame/fx_test.clj :226, 232-247; implementation/test/re_frame/cross_spec_cljs_test.cljs :182-185.
- The epoch summariser (implementation/src/re_frame/epoch.cljc :273, 287, 299) maps :rf.fx/skipped-on-platform onto :outcome :skipped-on-platform.

Note: the bead said chapter 08 "Three v1 surfaces removed" lists :platforms #{:server} and :rf.fx/skipped-on-platform. A grep of docs/guide/08-from-re-frame-v1.md shows it does NOT name them (confined to chapters 03 and 07). So no doc fix is required either — the audit-bead's chapter pointer was loose.

Closing as no-op verified.

title: Guide ch.04 :test preset mismatch with impl (rf2-x13a follow-up)
description:
Chapter 04 (Frames) describes the :test preset as adding :platforms #{:test}. The actual expand-preset in implementation/src/re_frame/frame.cljc defines :test as {:fx-overrides {:http :rf.test/http-stub}} — no :platforms key.

Guide-vs-impl mismatch. Either:
1. Update the guide to match impl: :test sets :fx-overrides for HTTP stubs.
2. Update impl to also set :platforms #{:test} (if that's the intended semantic).

Determine intent first by re-reading Spec 002 §Frame presets (or wherever :test is described normatively). Whichever direction the spec says, fix the other to match.

Discovered from rf2-x13a guide audit.

title: Please simplify the public documentation map and clarify ownership boundaries
description: The review found that the front-door documentation still asks readers to absorb multiple navigation taxonomies, while the ownership matrix still leaves some surfaces spread across several specs. Please simplify the public entry path and tighten ownership wording so there is a more obvious canonical home for views, subscriptions, and related companion material. This would make the documentation system reflect the same simplicity goals that the runtime design emphasizes.
notes:
Constraint (Mike, 2026-05-09): README's narrative is OFF LIMITS. Don't restructure it. Any additions to README for doc-map simplification go at the BOTTOM under a new section ## Information for AIs.

## Updated scope

### Concern 1 — front-door tiering

Don't change docs/guide/README.md's voice or spec/README.md's content. Don't tier 'human entry' vs 'AI entry' by editing the existing READMEs.

DO add a single new section at the bottom of repo root README.md titled ## Information for AIs. Content:
- A compact bulleted reading order for AI implementers / spec checkers / porting agents.
- 'Spec is the artefact' restated, with a direct pointer to spec/README.md.
- 'Pattern docs are worked examples; spec/* is normative; docs/guide/ is narrative.'
- Cross-link to spec/Conventions.md for naming / packaging / ownership rules.
- Maybe a short cheat-sheet: 'where does <surface> live?' → spec/Ownership.md.

This section is the AI-readable map the rest of the repo doesn't currently have. Narrative readers can skip it; the heading announces 'this is for tools'.

### Concern 2 — Ownership tighten

Spec 'canonical home' for each surface tightened. Non-canonical references in other specs become brief cross-links. This concern is unaffected by the README constraint — it's all spec-level.

## Out of scope

- Don't edit README's existing prose (the narrative).
- Don't restructure docs/guide/.
- Don't change spec/README.md's existing prose.
- Don't introduce new doc tiers (e.g. 'Tier 1 / Tier 2'). The narrative-vs-AI split is what we have.

title: Please refresh the example catalog and example run stories
description: The example index omits some shipped companion examples, and some example READMEs advertise run paths or local support material that the repo does not currently provide. Please update the example catalog, cross-links, and run instructions so readers land on the smallest faithful example first and do not discover missing build paths mid-flow. That would make the companion corpus more self-contained for both humans and porting agents.

title: Repo-wide sweep for broken cross-links in docs + code
description:
Sweep every Markdown file and source file in the repo for broken cross-references. Several recent reorgs (rf2-9hft promoted docs/specification/ -> spec/; rf2-heo2 removed get-view; rf2-d0pi swept reg-view; rf2-n4um dropped h; etc.) likely left stale links and anchor references in their wake.

## Scope

Markdown files:
- README.md
- spec/*.md (all)
- spec/conformance/README.md
- docs/guide/*.md (all chapters)
- examples/*/README.md
- implementation/README.md
- CLAUDE.md
- Pattern-*.md
- Any other .md surfaced by find

Code-level path/anchor references:
- Source-file docstrings that mention paths (e.g. 'see docs/specification/...').
- Comments referencing spec sections.
- Any test-fixture file path that's hard-coded.

## What's a 'broken' link

- File paths that don't resolve (e.g. ../specification/X.md after the promote-to-spec rename).
- Anchor references (#some-section) where the section heading was renamed or removed.
- External URLs that 404 (low priority — only flag obvious dead links like to-be-rewritten github URLs).
- References to symbols that have been removed (e.g. rf/get-view, rf/h).

## Process

1. Use a markdown link checker if available (lychee, markdown-link-check). Or write a small CLJS / bb script that parses each .md, extracts links/anchors, validates them.
2. For source-code references, grep for known stale paths:
   - 'docs/specification/' -> should be 'spec/' now (rf2-9hft)
   - '/get-view' -> should be '/view' (rf2-heo2)
   - 'rf/h ' or '(h ' -> removed (rf2-n4um)
   - 'reg-view :' (keyword form) -> defn-shape now (rf2-d0pi)
   - 're-frame.alpha' -> dissolved (rf2-7cb2)
3. For each broken reference, fix in place. Where the target exists under a new path/name, redirect. Where the target was removed entirely, remove the reference.
4. Where the broken link is in a closed bead description / historical notes — leave alone (don't rewrite history).

## Output

A report listing broken links found + fixed (one PR with all the fixes).

## Sequencing

Land AFTER all the in-flight reorgs (rf2-4v73, rf2-kdvh, rf2-zuwz, etc.) so the sweep catches everything in one pass. Otherwise the sweep gets stale immediately.

If any in-flight work surfaces ADDITIONAL broken-link patterns we don't know about (e.g. an agent moves files we didn't anticipate), this sweep catches them.

## Out of scope

- Don't restructure docs (that's other beads).
- Don't add new cross-references; only fix broken ones.

## Closes / related

- Catches drift from rf2-9hft, rf2-heo2, rf2-d0pi, rf2-n4um, rf2-7cb2, etc.
- Adjacent to rf2-au1b (Playwright final-pass) — both are end-of-cycle verification beads.

title: Final-pass: Playwright verifies every example end-to-end after all reorgs land
description:
Final verification pass via Playwright. After all the example-related reorganisations and migrations land, run the full Playwright suite against every example and confirm end-to-end behaviour holds.

## Goal

Empirical proof that every example works as a user-facing app, not just that it compiles. Each example's Playwright spec exercises the golden path + at least one edge case, asserts user-visible state.

## Scope

Run npm run test:examples and verify ALL specs pass:

- counter
- todomvc
- realworld (post-rf2-o8t6 Spec 014 retrofit)
- login (post-rf2-wdu8 :http -> :rf.http/managed)
- nine-states
- routing
- managed-http-counter
- ssr
- state-machine-walkthrough
- 7guis: temperature, flight-booker, timer, crud, circle-drawer, cells

If any spec fails:
1. Identify root cause: example drift / spec drift / framework regression.
2. Fix in place.
3. Re-run.
4. Repeat until 100% pass rate.

## Success criteria

- All Playwright specs pass on a clean main checkout.
- No timer-style flakes (run 3-5 times to confirm stability).
- The orchestrator (implementation/scripts/serve-and-run-examples-tests.cjs) discovers every spec.
- Test report shows N specs run / N passed (no skipped, no flaked).

## Out of scope

- Don't add new specs; only verify existing ones.
- Don't refactor specs; if they need rewrites, file follow-up beads.
- Don't introduce new example-time behaviours; only verify documented behaviours.

## Sequencing — at the END

Land AFTER:
- rf2-4v73 (realworld test harness extraction)
- rf2-kdvh (playwright spec-per-example move)
- rf2-zuwz (examples best-practice audit)
- rf2-o8t6 (realworld Spec 014 retrofit)
- rf2-wdu8 (HTTP migration sweep)
- rf2-kx74 (examples per-substrate reorg, if it lands first)
- rf2-3yij / rf2-2qit (substrate adapters, if those land)

Effectively: this bead is the final-pass before a v1.0 cut, validating that every example demonstrates correct re-frame2 idioms in working state.

## Closes / related

- Sibling to rf2-zuwz (audit) but behavior-focused, not structure-focused.
- Last-line-of-defense check before cutting any release.

title: examples/ HTTP migration: sweep remaining :http effects to :rf.http/managed
description:
Some examples still use the legacy :http effect (and :http.canned-success / :http.canned-failure test stubs). Sweep them to the Spec 014 :rf.http/managed surface.

## Audit

grep -rn ':http\b\|:http\.canned\|:http\.demo' examples/

Per-example status hypothesis:
- examples/login/ — uses :http and :http.canned-success per rf2-w3vn report.
- examples/realworld/ — has demo-stub registered per rf2-w3vn; rf2-o8t6 covers the broader retrofit.
- examples/managed_http_counter/ — already uses :rf.http/managed (the canonical Spec 014 demo).
- Other examples — verify by grep.

## Replacement

| Legacy | Replacement |
|---|---|
| :http | :rf.http/managed |
| :http.canned-success | :rf.http/managed-canned-success |
| :http.canned-failure | :rf.http/managed-canned-failure |
| :http.demo-stub (custom) | replace with managed + a documented stub server, or use canned-success in test paths |

## Coordination with rf2-o8t6

rf2-o8t6 is the realworld-specific Spec 014 retrofit. This bead is broader — covers login + any other examples not handled by rf2-o8t6. Sequence:
1. rf2-o8t6 lands realworld first (canonical Spec 014 demo).
2. This bead sweeps remaining stragglers (login, anything else surfaced by grep).

If overlap is small, this bead might fold into rf2-o8t6's PR. If broader, separate PR.

## Out of scope

- Don't touch managed_http_counter — it's already canonical.
- Don't add new examples; only sweep existing ones.

## Verify

After sweep: npm run test:examples — all Playwright specs should pass.

## Sequencing

Land AFTER rf2-o8t6 (Spec 014 retrofit of realworld). If standalone (other examples have :http drift but realworld retrofit is happy), can land independently.

## Closes / related

- Sibling to rf2-o8t6.
- Caught by rf2-zuwz (examples best-practice audit) but worth a dedicated sweep bead because the migration shape is uniform.

title: examples/playwright/ is misnamed — move specs into their respective example dirs
description:
examples/playwright/ is structurally odd. It's not an example; it houses Playwright spec files for the example apps. The dir-name suggests 'this is an example called playwright', which it isn't.

## Recommended fix

Option A: spec-per-example. Each spec moves into the example it tests:

  examples/counter/counter.spec.cjs
  examples/todomvc/todomvc.spec.cjs
  examples/seven_guis/cells/cells.spec.cjs
  ... etc.

Self-contained: example source + spec + README all in one dir. The orchestrator (implementation/scripts/serve-and-run-examples-tests.cjs) globs examples/*/*.spec.cjs (or examples/**/*.spec.cjs depending on nesting).

Alternative (Option B): top-level tests/e2e/ for all Playwright specs. Less self-contained but cleaner separation. Use only if Option A creates orchestrator complexity that's not worth the everyday navigation win.

## Sweep

- Move every spec file from examples/playwright/<name>.spec.cjs to its example dir.
- Update implementation/scripts/serve-and-run-examples-tests.cjs to glob the new locations.
- Update any package.json scripts that reference the old paths.
- Update any docs (README, examples/README.md if present) that link to the spec dir.

## Coordination with rf2-kx74

rf2-kx74 (examples reorg into per-substrate) was going to keep examples/playwright/ but tier it by substrate. That preserves the misnaming. Better: combine these two beads — the per-substrate reorg AND the spec-per-example move land together:

  examples/
    reagent/
      counter/
        core.cljs
        counter.spec.cjs
      todomvc/
        core.cljs
        todomvc.spec.cjs
    uix/
      counter/
        core.cljs
        counter.spec.cjs

If Mike wants this combined with rf2-kx74, this bead becomes a sub-task of that one. If standalone (cheap immediate cleanup before any substrate reorg), it ships first.

## Out of scope

- Don't change spec content; only move location.
- Don't delete examples/playwright/ until every spec has been moved + orchestrator updated + tests pass.

## Sequencing

Two paths:
1. Standalone: land before rf2-kx74. Cheap mechanical move; Mike sees the cleaner layout immediately.
2. Combined with rf2-kx74: pairs with substrate reorg; one PR.

Mike's call. (1) is faster; (2) is one less reshuffle.

title: Substrate adapter: Helix
description:
Add Helix as a Spec 006 substrate adapter, parallel to Reagent and UIx. Smaller scope than the UIx adapter — Helix has fewer users and serves the 'absolute minimal React wrapper' niche.

## Scope

### Adapter at re-frame.substrate.helix

Implement the Spec 006 adapter contract for Helix. Same shape as the UIx adapter:
- Subscription via useSyncExternalStore-wrapping hook.
- Component-local state via useState / useRef.
- Render scheduler delegated to React.
- Context via react/createContext + useContext.
- (rf/init! helix-adapter/adapter) at app entry.

### reg-view emission

Parallel macro: reg-view-helix that emits defnc. Same reasoning as UIx — separate macro per substrate; combine later only if necessary.

### Smaller curated example port

Smallest viable demonstration:
- counter

That's enough to prove the substrate adapter works. If demand surfaces, expand later.

## Triggers / sequencing

- Depends on rf2-kx74 (examples reorg) being settled — examples/helix/counter/ goes in the new substrate-tiered layout.
- Should land AFTER the UIx adapter so the Spec 006 contract has been exercised by two substrates and clear gaps surface.

## Cost estimate

~3-5 days of agent work. Adapter is similar shape to UIx; the example sweep is much smaller.

## Closes / related

- Reinforces the 'spec is substrate-agnostic' claim with a third reference.
- Pairs with rf2-kx74 (examples reorg) and the UIx-adapter bead.

## Why P3 (not P2)

UIx covers the modern-hooks-CLJS-React audience. Helix is the further validation, not the load-bearing port. Schedule when capacity allows; not blocking.
notes:
rf2-z9n1 resolved as (a)+(α) [2026-05-09]: same DOM-injection mandate as UIx (per rf2-3yij note). Helix adapter MUST ship :data-rf2-source-coord injection. See rf2-z7f7.

---

## Decisions (Mike, 2026-05-10)

Direction: port the UIx adapter (rf2-3yij PR #151) to Helix. All 8 UIx decisions transfer (same React/hooks substrate model):

1. Hook naming: `use-subscribe`.
2. Frame propagation: shared `re-frame.substrate.context` (already factored).
3. No auto-inject — components call `use-subscribe` directly.
4. `reg-view` macro stays Reagent-only; Helix users call `reg-view*`.
5. Source-coord DOM annotation via adapter wrap.
6. Render flush: `flush-views!` wrapping React's `act()`.
7. Smoke-test set: counter + login.
8. Helix version target: latest (1.x line). No back-compat.

Pre-alpha — no migration concerns.
close_reason: Already merged via PR #176. Helix substrate adapter shipped.

title: examples/ reorganisation: per-substrate top-level dirs (paired with first non-Reagent substrate)
description:
When the first non-Reagent substrate ships (UIx most likely; possibly Helix), reorganise examples/ into per-substrate top-level dirs.

## Trigger

Don't dispatch until the substrate-adapter bead is also being scheduled. The rename is paired with the new substrate's first canonical example so it lands as one consistent move; otherwise it's premature churn.

## Target layout

  examples/
    reagent/
      counter/
      todomvc/
      realworld/
      seven_guis/
      state_machine_walkthrough/
      nine_states/
      routing/
      ssr/
      managed_http_counter/
      login/
    <new-substrate>/
      counter/
      todomvc/
      managed-http-counter/
      frame-provider-demo/
      (curated subset; not 1:1 mirror)
    playwright/
      reagent/
        <existing specs moved here>
      <new-substrate>/
        <new specs>

Helix gets a third dir only if real demand surfaces.

## Sweep

- Move all existing examples/<name>/ into examples/reagent/<name>/.
- Move examples/playwright/ specs into examples/playwright/reagent/.
- Update implementation/scripts/serve-and-run-examples-tests.cjs path entries.
- Update implementation/shadow-cljs.edn build target paths.
- Update docs that link to examples (README, guide chapters, spec docs that reference examples).
- Update any cross-references in code (test files, example READMEs).

## Process

1. First commit: rename existing examples to examples/reagent/. Sweep references.
2. Second commit: add examples/<new-substrate>/counter/ as the first port — proves the substrate adapter works end-to-end.
3. Build out the new-substrate set incrementally over follow-up beads.

## Constraints

- DON'T share view code across substrates initially. Duplication is honest about substrate differences.
- DO share events/subs/fx if they're 100% substrate-agnostic (and don't import Reagent / UIx / Helix). For the v1.0 phase, prefer self-contained trees over clever cljc/per-substrate-views layouts.
- DON'T 1:1 mirror every Reagent example. Curated subset per non-canonical substrate.

## Cross-references

- rf2-vf8m (long-term repo split) — orthogonal but may interact if the substrate adapters ship as separate artefacts.
- Future bead: feat: substrate adapter for UIx (when filed, that bead is the trigger to schedule this reorg).

## Out of scope

- Don't touch implementation/ or docs/specification/ layouts. Examples-only reorg.
- Don't pre-organise before a non-Reagent substrate is greenlit.
close_reason: Resolved via PR #160 — examples reorganised under per-substrate top-level dirs (Reagent + UIx) (commits 3c80cc1, 4a0bf2c, 9acde89, d9a0479, 1d0d5e3).

title: [bug] reg-event-* silently ignores :interceptors when given as metadata-key inside the metadata-map
description:
Surfaced by rf2-w3vn during 7guis #6 (Circle Drawer) work. The example had  placed inside a metadata-map (the docstring/metadata position of reg-event-db), where it was silently ignored — undo/redo was effectively never enabled until the agent recognised the typo and moved interceptors to the positional slot.

Two paths:
1. Lint/warn at registration time when :interceptors appears in the metadata-map. Compile-time warning if possible; runtime warning otherwise.
2. Honor :interceptors in the metadata-map AS WELL as the positional slot. Same key, two valid positions.

Path 1 is the surface-shrinking choice (one canonical position). Path 2 is the user-friendly choice (Postel's law — accept either).

Recommend path 1: warn at registration. Either is mechanical to implement.

Discovered from rf2-w3vn (PR #86).

title: examples/realworld: extract inline tests to a proper test harness
description:
examples/realworld/ has tests mixed inline with the normal application code. Cleanup task: move tests to a proper test harness directory and verify they still pass.

## Scope

1. Audit examples/realworld/ for inline test code:
   - deftest forms inside source ns
   - testing/comment blocks containing assertions
   - Any (clojure.test/...) require in production source

2. Move them to a sibling test directory (likely examples/realworld/test/realworld/ or similar — match existing structure of other examples that already have tests).

3. Set up the test runner config (whatever the realworld example uses for shadow-cljs / clj):
   - shadow-cljs.edn build target for tests if not already present
   - test-runner script wiring
   - Confirm the test discovery path includes the new directory

4. Verify all moved tests pass post-relocation.

5. Source ns becomes test-free; production code only.

## Why now

rf2-o8t6 (rewrite realworld to use :rf.http/managed) is queued. Doing the test-harness cleanup first means the rewrite agent works against a clean separation of concerns and doesn't have to navigate inline test scaffolding. Order: rf2-w3vn (Phase 3 — realworld example exists) -> THIS bead (test harness) -> rf2-o8t6 (Spec 014 retrofit).

## Verification

After the move:
- examples/realworld/ source files contain no clojure.test references.
- Tests run via the documented runner; pass.
- Examples Playwright suite (the headless smoke test) still runs.

## Out of scope

- Don't write new tests; only relocate existing ones.
- Don't change test semantics or signatures.
- Don't touch other examples (counter, todomvc, etc. have their own conventions).

## Closes / related

- Prerequisite to rf2-o8t6.
- Blocked by rf2-w3vn (Phase 3 — realworld must exist as a runnable example first).

title: RFC 2119 Contract block pilot in 002-Frames.md (resolves rf2-gpva)
description:
Per rf2-gpva Option B: pilot RFC 2119 normative phrasing in 002-Frames.md. Place the Contract block at the END of the doc (not the top) so the narrative reader keeps the existing reading experience.

## Scope (deliberately narrow)

Only docs/specification/002-Frames.md is touched. Other specs stay in narrative voice for now.

## Add a §Normative Requirements section at the end

Sketch:

  ## Normative Requirements

  This section enumerates the contract that an implementation MUST satisfy
  to claim conformance with Spec 002. The narrative above provides the
  rationale; the bullets below are the contract.

  Definitions (per RFC 2119):
  - MUST / REQUIRED / SHALL — absolute requirement.
  - MUST NOT / SHALL NOT — absolute prohibition.
  - SHOULD / RECOMMENDED — there may exist valid reasons in particular circumstances to deviate, but the full implications must be understood.
  - SHOULD NOT — same, in the negative.
  - MAY / OPTIONAL — truly optional; conformance-neutral.

  ### N-1. Frame-id is a keyword
  An implementation MUST treat frame-ids as keywords. Conformance: dispatch-envelope.edn

  ### N-2. Default frame is :rf/default
  An implementation MUST use :rf/default as the frame-id when no frame is specified. Conformance: dispatch-envelope.edn

  ... (continue extracting normative claims from the narrative above)

The agent's job:
1. Read 002-Frames.md end-to-end and extract every claim that's normative (where 'normative' means an implementation must do this thing).
2. Classify each as MUST / SHOULD / MAY based on:
   - MUST: contract-breaking if not honoured. e.g. dispatch envelope shape, drain semantics, frame isolation.
   - SHOULD: strongly recommended; deviation needs justification. e.g. dispatch ordering preferences, cofx convention.
   - MAY: optional or conformance-neutral. e.g. additional metadata slots, performance optimizations.
3. Write each as a numbered N-X bullet with one short paragraph + cross-link to its conformance fixture.
4. Add a §Definitions header explaining the keywords (per RFC 2119 §1).
5. Footnote-link each normative claim in the narrative back to its Contract entry: e.g. paragraphs that say 'the dispatch envelope shape' get a [N-1] footnote.

## Cross-references

- Conformance fixtures under docs/specification/conformance/fixtures/ — many already exist for Spec 002. Cross-link each Contract entry to the fixture(s) that pin it. Fixtures may need a small metadata addition (a :rf.spec/normative-id slot) so the cross-reference is mechanical. If that's heavy, just put the fixture filenames in the Contract entry's text.

## Out of scope

- Other spec docs stay in narrative voice. Only 002-Frames.md is touched.
- DO NOT touch 000-Vision.md or any spec-as-argument doc.
- Don't sweep cross-references in OTHER specs that point at 002-Frames; their existing references should still work because the narrative-side anchors don't move.

## Verification

- Read 002-Frames.md end-to-end after the edit. Narrative reading experience preserved (no Contract block at the top jarring the reader).
- §Normative Requirements section is mechanically grep-able for normative-id (N-1, N-2, ...).
- Each Contract entry has a conformance-fixture link.
- Spell-check, link-check.

## Deliverable

PR titled 'spec: 002-Frames Contract block (RFC 2119 pilot, rf2-gpva)'. No AI attribution.

After merge, observe for a few weeks. If observed value is real (no new ambiguity beads against 002, conformance-fixture cross-referencing is useful), file follow-up beads to scale to other specs (rf2-rfc2119-004-views, etc.). If no value, leave the pilot in place but don't scale.

## Closes / related

- Resolves rf2-gpva (decision: Option B, pilot in one doc with Contract block at the end).
- Future scale-up beads will be filed only if the pilot earns its keep.
notes: Reviewer §6 recommendation applied at PR https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/137 (rf2-04sm). 11 keeper clauses landed in 000-Vision; 7 addressee-flipped clauses moved to a new spec/SPEC-AUTHORING.md (SA-N ids); 31 paraphrase / premature-normative clauses dropped; 2 (C-000.06, C-000.35) held back pending spec-002 / spec-009 ratification per the reviewer's §5 flag.
close_reason: Decision recorded (refine narrowly via rf2-uuoj review; applied via rf2-04sm / PR #137 merged); don't scale to other specs without empirical-audit driving it. See findings/rf2-ehml-spec000-contract-draft.md + findings/rf2-ehml-review.md.

title: Migration prompt: scan target project's deps and bump to latest released re-frame2
description:
The migration agent prompt should include a deps-update step. When migrating a v1 project to v2, examine the target's deps and update the re-frame coordinate to the latest released version of re-frame2 (if a release is available).

## Scope

The migration prompt likely lives in MIGRATION.md (or a separate agent-instructions file). Verify; check whether a deps-update step is already present.

If absent, add a step that instructs the agent to:

1. Inspect the target project's dependency files. Common shapes:
   - deps.edn — look in :deps and any :aliases ../:extra-deps for re-frame coordinates.
   - project.clj — look in :dependencies.
   - shadow-cljs.edn — look in :dependencies.
   - bb.edn — for Babashka tooling.
2. Identify any re-frame coordinate. Common shapes today:
   - re-frame/re-frame {:mvn/version "1.x.x"}
   - re-frame {:mvn/version "1.x.x"}  (older shorter form)
   - [re-frame "1.x.x"]  (lein vector form)
3. Look up the latest released re-frame2 version. Source TBD — once published, likely Maven Central / clojars. The agent's heuristic:
   - If the artefact label has been resolved (rf2-5sqd) to re-frame/re-frame v2.x: bump the version field to the latest v2.x.
   - If the artefact has been published as day8/re-frame-2: replace the coord entirely.
4. If no released re-frame2 version is available yet (pre-publication): leave the dep alone but flag in the migration report that the user must manually update once a release lands.

## Coupling with rf2-5sqd

This bead's wording depends on the rf2-5sqd outcome (artefact name). Either resolve in this bead's text up-front, or leave parameterised and update after rf2-5sqd lands.

## Sequencing

Should land after rf2-5sqd (so the prompt can name the actual coord). Until then, file with a placeholder / leave OPEN.

If pre-rf2-5sqd-decision, the bead's resolution is just 'add the step in a way that handles either artefact-name outcome' — i.e. detect re-frame at any of the known coord shapes, replace with whatever the published v2 artefact ends up being.
notes:
rf2-5sqd resolved (2026-05-09): artefact is day8/re-frame-2. The migration prompt's deps-bump step now has a concrete target:

- Detect any of: re-frame/re-frame {:mvn/version ...}, [re-frame "..."], re-frame {:mvn/version ...}.
- Replace with day8/re-frame-2 {:mvn/version <latest-released>}.
- If no released v2 yet, leave dep alone and flag in migration report.

title: Remove get-view entirely; view is canonical (resolves rf2-sx7t)
description:
Per rf2-sx7t Option 3: remove get-view entirely. view is the only runtime-lookup handle. Pre-1.0; no deprecation period needed.

## Removals

- implementation/src/re_frame/core.cljc — remove the get-view fn (currently aliased to view). Keep only view.
- implementation/src/re_frame/views.cljs (or wherever the underlying lookup lives) — if get-view exists there separately, remove and ensure view is the implementation entry.

## Sweep references

- docs/specification/004-Views.md — §How registered views are used in hiccup, §get-view section heading, §Alternative forms, §The h macro (already gone if rf2-u33b lands first), §Hot-reload behaviour for re-registered views.
- docs/specification/Construction-Prompts.md — examples (rf/get-view ...) in CP scaffolds.
- docs/specification/AI-Audit.md — references in views-call-syntax discussion.
- docs/specification/API.md — drop the get-view row entirely; update the view row to drop the 'alias of get-view' parenthetical.
- Any other doc referencing get-view — grep -rn 'get-view' docs/.

## Code sweep

- grep -rn 'get-view' implementation/ examples/ — every match is a candidate. Convert to view.
- Tests using get-view — convert.

## MIGRATION

This is internal to re-frame2's pre-1.0 evolution. No public migration entry needed unless we want to document the alias removal for early adopters tracking the unstable API.

## Sequencing

Independent. Touches files largely orthogonal to the in-flight work. Land any time.

## Closes / related

- Resolves rf2-sx7t.
- Same surface-shrinking principle as rf2-7cb2, rf2-n4um, rf2-iyzm, rf2-d0pi: one canonical name; no aliases or deprecations.

title: Reconcile get-view vs view across spec and source (rf2-yl9n follow-up)
description:
rf2-yl9n introduced (re-frame.core/view id) as the runtime-lookup handle for registered views, per the rf2-iyzm Form-2 resolution. The implementation adds `view` as an alias of the existing `get-view`, and the spec corpus documents `view` in the new normative sections (Spec 001 §query API, Spec 004 §Calling a registered view, Conventions §Render-tree shape vs runtime lookup, Cross-Spec-Interactions §21).

The pre-existing `get-view` references throughout the spec corpus and source were NOT swept in rf2-yl9n. They remain valid (the function is unchanged) but the corpus now describes two names for the same handle, which is drift waiting to happen.

## Scope

Decide one of:

1. `view` is the canonical name; `get-view` is the deprecated alias (keep working, mark as deprecated, sweep references).
2. `view` and `get-view` are interchangeable aliases (document as such; pick one for examples; leave both indexed in API.md).
3. `view` is the canonical name; `get-view` is removed in a future major.

Once decided, sweep:

- docs/specification/004-Views.md — §"How registered views are used in hiccup", §"get-view — the canonical post-registration lookup", §"Alternative forms", §"The h macro", §"Resolved decisions §The h macro's expansion strategy", §"Hot-reload behaviour for re-registered views"
- docs/specification/Construction-Prompts.md — examples (rf/get-view ...) in CP scaffolds
- docs/specification/AI-Audit.md — references in the views-call-syntax discussion
- docs/specification/API.md — current row reads "view (alias of get-view)"; collapse to one row reflecting the chosen canon
- implementation/src/re_frame/core.cljc — the docstrings on `get-view` and `view`

## Sequencing

After rf2-yl9n PR merges. Mechanical sweep; no design uncertainty once Mike picks one of the three options.

## Discovered from

rf2-yl9n implementation. The original task's bead text spelled the lookup as `(rf/view id)` while the spec corpus had `(rf/get-view :id)` everywhere. rf2-yl9n added `view` as the documented form and an aliased fn; this bead reconciles the rest.
notes:
Resolved (Mike, 2026-05-08): Option 3 — remove get-view entirely. Per Mike: 'This is a new code base so we don't have to worry about deprecating anything.'

view is canonical and only. get-view is removed from impl + spec corpus. Implementation bead filed.

title: [bug] :sub-runs :result-changed? always true when sub recomputed
description:
Surfaced by re-frame-pair2 (rf2-9ra3) during port. The :sub-runs projection in :rf/epoch-record records :result-changed? for each sub recomputation — but the value is always true when the sub recomputed. The flag carries no information beyond 'this sub ran'.

Likely the value-equal recompute suppression (rf2-719e) handles the not-changed case by suppressing the :sub/run trace entirely — so any :sub-runs entry IS a changed result by construction. The :result-changed? slot is dead.

Either:
1. Remove :result-changed? from :sub-runs entries (it's structurally always true under current semantics).
2. Re-purpose :result-changed? to carry useful info (e.g., :input-changed? distinguishing 'input-graph fired but value-equal' vs 'inputs propagated novel values').
3. Document that :result-changed? is structurally true under current contract; consumers can drop the slot.

Discovered from re-frame-pair2 STATUS.md.

title: [bug] :effects projection captures only warning/error outcomes — successful fx attribution requires walking :trace-events
description:
Surfaced by re-frame-pair2 (rf2-9ra3) during port. The :rf/epoch-record :effects projection currently records only warning/error outcomes per fx; successful fx executions don't appear in the projection. Consumers wanting per-event :effects attribution have to walk the raw :trace-events stream and re-derive.

Either:
1. Make :effects record success outcomes too (trace event → projection entry on every dispatched fx, regardless of outcome).
2. Document the projection's current contract explicitly: success is implicit; only failure surfaces. Add a separate projection or helper for users wanting full attribution.

Discovered from re-frame-pair2 STATUS.md.

Sequencing: independent of in-flight work. Affects Tool-Pair contract — coordinate with rf2-piag (:render-key tuple) if both touch :rf/epoch-record's projections.

title: Spec-Schemas: :get-event-arg signature describes path-vector but impl + corpus use single-key
description:
Spec-Schemas.md (rf/handler-body-dsl) describes :get-event-arg as: [:tuple [:= :get-event-arg] :int [:vector :any]] -- (get-in (nth event N) path).

But the conformance impl (re-frame.conformance/resolve-value) and the conformance corpus (ssr-hydrate.edn) use the single-key form: [:get-event-arg N :key], not a path vector. After rf2-xb5o lands, the canonical form is [:get-event-arg N :key] / [:get-event-arg N :key default-val].

Update Spec-Schemas.md so the schema and prose match the impl + corpus + README:
  [:tuple [:= :get-event-arg] :int :keyword]                            ;; [:get-event-arg N :key]
  [:tuple [:= :get-event-arg] :int :keyword :any]                       ;; [:get-event-arg N :key default-val]

And the prose row: '(get (nth event N) :key)' or 'with optional default-val'.

Surfaced during rf2-xb5o implementation.

title: Conformance DSL: split :event-arg key-access into :get-event-arg (resolves rf2-pz9f)
description:
Per rf2-pz9f Option (a): make :event-arg's 3rd element always default-for-nil. The keyword-key-access overload moves to the existing :get-event-arg op.

## Changes

### Fixture migration

grep -rn '\[:event-arg [0-9] :' docs/specification/conformance/fixtures/ — every match is a candidate for key-access. Verify intent (read each fixture; if it expects to extract a key from a map result, migrate; if it expects a keyword as default-for-nil, leave).

Known sites:
- ssr-hydration-mismatch.edn — [:event-arg 1 :rf/app-db] migrates to [:get-event-arg 1 :rf/app-db].

NOT a migration:
- routing-navigate.edn — [:event-arg 2 {}] is {} as default; non-keyword 3rd never triggered key-access. Stays.

### Impl change

implementation/src/re_frame_2/conformance.cljc (or wherever resolve-value lives — find via grep) — remove the type-dispatch branch on :event-arg's 3rd arg. The 3rd arg is now always default-for-nil. :get-event-arg is the only path for key-access.

### Docs

docs/specification/conformance/README.md — document both forms explicitly:
- [:event-arg n] / [:event-arg n default-val]: default-for-nil only.
- [:get-event-arg n :key] / [:get-event-arg n :key default-val]: key-access (with optional default).

Remove any prose describing the prior runtime-type-dispatch overload.

### Tests

- Existing tests that exercise :event-arg with keyword 3rd-arg expecting key-access: migrate to :get-event-arg.
- Add fixture asserting [:event-arg 0 :some-keyword] returns the keyword as default (no longer key-access) when the resolved arg is a map containing that key.
- Add fixture asserting [:get-event-arg 0 :some-key] does the key-access.

## Sequencing

Independent of in-flight work. Touches conformance corpus + DSL impl + README. No conflict with rebase-in-flight (rf2-d0pi/hbt1/u33b/s9dn) or pair-skill agents.

## Closes / related

- Resolves rf2-pz9f.
- Aligns with the session's surface-shrinking pattern (explicit over overloaded).

title: Drop h macro — sweep call sites, remove from Spec 004, MIGRATION (resolves rf2-n4um)
description:
Per rf2-n4um Option C: drop the h macro entirely. Late-binding via (rf/view :id) is the canonical handle for the cases h was solving.

## Removals

- implementation/src/re_frame/views_macros.clj (or .cljc) — delete the h macro definition
- implementation/src/re_frame/core.cljc — remove h from public exports / re-exports
- Any helper fns the h macro relies on (only if no longer used elsewhere — grep before deleting)

## Sweep

grep -rn '(h \[\|(h\s' implementation/ examples/ docs/ — find every h call site.

For each:
- Plain hiccup with namespaced view keyword: convert to Var-ref form
  (h [:div [:my-app/widget arg]]) -> [:div [my-app/widget arg]]
- Cases that genuinely needed late-binding (id-based lookup): convert to (rf/view :id)
  (h [:my-app/widget arg]) -> [(rf/view :my-app/widget) arg]
- HTML-only h calls: drop the h wrapper entirely; the inner hiccup is unchanged
  (h [:div [:p ...]]) -> [:div [:p ...]]

Most call sites are likely the first form (Var-ref conversion). The (rf/view ...) substitution is rare — only where the user genuinely wants registry-id lookup at render time.

If a call site is ambiguous (the user's intent isn't clear from context), prefer Var-ref unless there's a comment indicating late-binding intent.

## Spec edits

- docs/specification/004-Views.md — remove §The h macro entirely. If the section anchor is referenced from elsewhere in the spec corpus, update those references.
- docs/specification/Cross-Spec-Interactions.md — verify nothing references h.
- docs/specification/Conventions.md — verify nothing references h.

## MIGRATION.md

Add entry:

  ## h macro removed (rf2-n4um)
  
  - (h [:div [:my-app/widget arg]]) -> [:div [my-app/widget arg]]    ; Var-ref form (most cases)
  - (h [:my-app/widget arg])         -> [(rf/view :my-app/widget) arg]  ; only if late-binding by id was the intent
  - (h [:div [:p ...]])              -> [:div [:p ...]]                ; HTML-only — drop the wrapper

## Tests

- Remove implementation/test/.../h_macro_test.* (or wherever the h tests live)
- Verify no other tests reference h directly (grep)

## Sequencing

Independent of in-flight work. Touches the h macro source, Spec 004, MIGRATION.md, plus example call sites. Examples currently being swept by other agents (rf2-d0pi merged into the d0pi branch); if rf2-d0pi has merged by the time this bead runs, base off main. If still in flight, consider stacking on rf2-d0pi or accepting small textual conflicts.

## Closes / related

- Resolves rf2-n4um (decision: drop).
- Aligns with rf2-7cb2 (drop alpha) and rf2-iyzm (Form 2 canonical) — same surface-shrinking principle.
- Does NOT affect (rf/view :id) lookup, which stays as the canonical late-binding handle.

title: Conformance corpus: add render capability for frame-provider fixtures
description:
rf2-sixo's bead description listed three conformance fixtures
(`frame-provider-establishes-context.edn`,
`frame-provider-nested-override.edn`,
`frame-provider-default-fallback.edn`) but the corpus is currently
host-agnostic pure-data event/sub/fx semantics — there's no
render-capability tag and no fixture-runner side that can mount a
Reagent component to verify React-context propagation.

Frame-provider is observable only at render time — the substrate puts
a keyword into React context; reg-view-registered descendants read it
via contextType during render. Without a render step in the harness,
the fixture has no observable.

Resolution options:

1. Add a `:reagent/render` capability tag and a CLJS-side fixture
   runner that `r/render`s the fixture's tree into a JSDOM mount,
   captures dispatches/subs run during render, compares.
2. Punt — accept that frame-provider is verified by CLJS unit tests
   (rf2-sixo lands these in `runtime_cljs_test.cljs`) and not by the
   conformance corpus. Document this gap in the conformance README.

Recommendation: option 2 short-term (rf2-sixo already shipped the unit
tests); option 1 long-term as ports beyond CLJS gain Reagent-equivalent
substrates and need to claim conformance.

Surfaces from rf2-sixo (the bead asked for fixtures the harness
can't run today).

title: build-frame-provider unused arg: flatten or document
description:
re-frame.views/build-frame-provider takes `_frame-keyword` at build
time but ignores it — the returned Reagent component takes the frame
keyword at render time, and a single built component services every
frame.

The substrate-side hook in re-frame.substrate.reagent/register-context-provider
currently calls (build-frame-provider frame-keyword) and discards the
arg. Two reasonable resolutions:

1. Flatten — change `build-frame-provider` to a 0-arity fn that
   returns the same generic component. Drop the unused arg from
   `register-context-provider` and update Spec 006's adapter pseudocode.
2. Honour the contract — make `build-frame-provider` return a
   per-frame specialised component (e.g., one whose first child is the
   frame-keyword baked in via a closure). This would let
   `frame-provider` write `[(build-frame-provider frame) child1 child2]`
   without re-passing the keyword.

Either resolution is fine; the current shape (arg present, ignored)
is the worst of both worlds because the ergonomics of `frame-provider`
require re-passing the keyword as the first arg to the inner fn.

Surfaces from rf2-sixo. Not blocking — `frame-provider` ships fine on
top of the current shape — but the substrate seam is a small wart that
deserves a deliberate decision.

Specs touched: 002 §What `frame-provider` is, 006 §Frame-provider via
React context.

title: Consider rolling README framing into docs/guide/01-why-re-frame2.md
description:
Mike (2026-05-08): the main README likely has wording / framing that's stronger than what's in docs/guide/01-why-re-frame2.md. Take a pass: read the README and the chapter side-by-side; lift framing where the README reads better; leave the chapter where it's already clear. Probably small edits.

## Process

1. Read README.md (re-frame2 root).
2. Read docs/guide/01-why-re-frame2.md.
3. Identify framing the chapter would benefit from — e.g., the elevator pitch, the comparison-vs-v1 framing, the architectural pillars, audience targeting.
4. Apply small edits to the chapter. Don't bulk-paste the README in — pick the sentences that work.

## Out of scope

- Rewriting the chapter wholesale (separate concern).
- Updating the README itself (unless drift surfaces — file as a separate bead).
- Sweeping the rest of the guide (rf2-x13a covers that).

## Cross-references

- Related to rf2-x13a (the broader docs/guide audit). This bead is content-improvement; rf2-x13a is drift-correction. They can land independently.

title: rf2-d0pi follow-up: sweep 7 missed files for old-shape reg-view
description:
rf2-d0pi (PR #54) swept the 11 spec/doc files enumerated in the bead. The agent then grepped for residual old-shape reg-view across docs/ and surfaced 7 more files containing old-shape (reg-view :keyword (fn [] ...)) calls that need converting:

- docs/specification/005-StateMachines.md
- docs/specification/007-Stories.md
- docs/specification/002-Frames.md
- docs/specification/Pattern-RemoteData.md
- docs/specification/Construction-Prompts.md
- docs/guide/02 (the views chapter — file may be docs/guide/02-something.md)
- docs/guide/04 (file may be docs/guide/04-views-and-frames.md or split)

Mechanical sweep — convert each (reg-view :keyword (fn [] ...)) to (reg-view sym [] ...). Drop explicit (rf/dispatcher) / (rf/subscriber) captures inside view bodies (auto-injected). Apply the metadata override only where the existing keyword diverges from (keyword (str *ns*) (str sym)).

After the sweep, verify: grep -rn 'reg-view :' docs/ returns zero matches in non-historical contexts (changelog/migration entries documenting the OLD shape are fine).

## Sequencing

Land AFTER rf2-d0pi (PR #54) merges. This is the cleanup pass.

## Coordination

rf2-hbt1 is in flight and rewrites docs/guide/05-state-machines.md and docs/specification/005-StateMachines.md. There's overlap with file 1 (005-StateMachines.md). When rf2-hbt1 lands, that file's reg-view shape is already correct — this bead's sweep skips it. Re-grep before doing the work to avoid double-touching.

title: Spec 014: re-order decode vs status-check — HTML 4xx misclassifies as decode-failure
description:
Surfaced by rf2-cfig (PR #53) while building the managed-http-counter Playwright example. The http-managed Phase 1 impl runs decode BEFORE the status check, so a 4xx response with an HTML body decoded as :json misclassifies as :rf.http/decode-failure rather than :rf.http/http-4xx.

The example worked around this by using :decode :text on the Fail button. Real users hitting a JSON API that returns HTML error pages on 4xx will see decode-failure traces and miss the real HTTP failure category.

Fix: status-check fires before decode. 4xx/5xx responses go straight to :rf.http/http-4xx / :rf.http/http-5xx without attempting decode. Decode runs only on 2xx (or whatever the spec says is success-eligible).

If decode SHOULD run on non-2xx for some reason (e.g. JSON error envelopes from an API that returns 200 with embedded error?), revisit Spec 014 §Failure categories and document the rule.

Touches: implementation/src/re_frame/http_managed.cljc — re-order the pipeline; tests in implementation/test/re_frame/http_managed_test.clj — add a fixture asserting HTML 404 → :rf.http/http-4xx (not decode-failure).

Discovered from rf2-cfig (PR #53).
notes:
Resolved (Mike, 2026-05-08): Option A — status-check fires before decode. 4xx/5xx responses route to :rf.http/http-4xx / :rf.http/http-5xx without attempting decode. Decode runs only on responses the status-check classifies as success-eligible (2xx by default).

Implementation: re-order the http-managed pipeline so status classification precedes decode. Update Spec 014 §Failure categories if the prose suggests otherwise. Add a fixture asserting HTML 404 → :rf.http/http-4xx (not decode-failure) — this is the explicit regression guard.

title: Spec edits formalising Form 2 as canonical view call syntax (resolves rf2-iyzm)
description:
Small follow-up to rf2-iyzm's resolution (Form 2 canonical). Adds explicit normative statements to the spec corpus.

## Edits

### docs/specification/004-Views.md

Add §'Calling a registered view':

> Render trees invoke a registered view by Var-reference: [my-view args]. This is the native Reagent component-call shape and the only supported render-time form. Keyword vectors at render time are HTML elements (Reagent's existing semantics) — the runtime does not intercept :keyword vectors and dispatch via the views registry.
>
> The keyword id assigned to a view by reg-view (or reg-view*) is reserved for runtime lookup and introspection: trace events, devtools, error frames, (rf/view id) lookups, registry-only views without a Var binding.

### docs/specification/Conventions.md

Add to the family-asymmetry section:

> Render trees use Vars; runtime lookups use ids. reg-view bridges them — auto-defs the symbol AND auto-derives the registry id. The same render/lookup split applies to reg-view*: it registers a fn under an id without a Var def; consumers retrieve it via (rf/view id) and inline it into render trees.

### docs/specification/001-Registration.md

Document (re-frame.core/view id) as the runtime-lookup handle for registered views. Returns the registered fn (whatever shape — Form-1, Form-2, etc.) or nil if not registered.

### docs/specification/Cross-Spec-Interactions.md

Add: 'Keyword vectors at render time are HTML elements, never views. The runtime does not intercept the keyword case — Reagent's hiccup semantics are preserved unmodified.'

## Tests

- A test/conformance fixture asserting that [:my-view args] in a render tree DOES NOT dispatch to a registered :my-view; it renders as a custom HTML element. (This is the negative-form test — proves the no-intercept policy.)
- A test asserting (rf/view :my.ns/my-view) returns the registered fn after (reg-view my-view [] body).

## Sequencing

Land AFTER rf2-d0pi merges. rf2-d0pi sweeps these same spec files; this bead's edits are additive to that work.

## Closes / related

- Resolves rf2-iyzm (decision recorded; this bead is the spec-write-out).
- Related to rf2-d0pi (which establishes the Var/id family-asymmetry; this bead formalises it for views in the spec).

title: TodoMVC: polish — simplify todo-input keydown, harden parse, explanatory comments
description:
Three small quality items surfaced during review.

1) todo-input over-engineered keydown: views.cljs:42-50 attaches keydown via native (.addEventListener node 'keydown' handle-keydown) and tags the DOM with __todomvcKeyHandler to support handler replacement on ref change. React synthetic :on-key-down handles preventDefault correctly — the manual setup is unnecessary complexity for an example.

Action: replace the bind-node + addEventListener machinery with :on-key-down handle-keydown directly on the [:input ...] map. The bind-node ref is still needed for stop's (set! (.-value node) '') behaviour; keep that, drop the event-listener part.

2) db/normalise-todo brittle on bad localStorage: db.cljs:11 (int id) throws if a user hand-edits localStorage to {id: 'abc'}. Wrap in (try (int id) (catch :default _ nil)) and (remove (comp nil? :id)) the result. The outer try/catch on parse already handles structural corruption — this just hardens against item-level corruption.

3) Explanatory comments for non-obvious choices:
- views.cljs:74-76 — :on-click + :readOnly true + :checked is React's controlled-checkbox pattern; one-line comment.
- views.cljs:66 — editing? local Reagent atom is the v1 TodoMVC tradeoff (per-component edit state vs single-source-of-truth in app-db); one-line note.

Spec compliance: not affected.

Affected files:
- examples/todomvc/views.cljs
- examples/todomvc/db.cljs
notes: PR #51: commit cba8b0b on todomvc-review-fixes. Awaiting merge.

title: Tool-Pair: extend elision-probe with epoch sentinels for parity (rf2-shjf follow-up)
description: rf2-shjf agent noted the elision probe (rf2-11hn) tracks trace/schema/registrar surfaces but doesn't yet exercise the epoch surface. Add sentinel strings + probe-namespace touches for re-frame.epoch's public surface so the bundle-grep test confirms epoch infra also elides in production. Small follow-up to keep the elision-probe coverage matrix complete.

title: Tool-Pair: misc surfaces — snapshot-of, sub-cache, :rf.registry/handler-replaced trace
description:
Smaller surfaces Tool-Pair lists that are not yet implemented:

1. **(rf/snapshot-of path opts)** — convenience query over the current frame's app-db at a path. Sugar but Tool-Pair pins it.
2. **(rf/sub-cache frame-id)** — CLJS-only inspection of a frame's sub cache contents (for pair tools to see what's currently subscribed and its cached value).
3. **:rf.registry/handler-replaced trace event** — per Spec 001 §Hot-reload semantics, fires when a re-registration replaces an existing handler. Probably already partially wired by rf2-hyxg agent; verify and fill the gap if not.

Production elision: snapshot-of is a public query (no elision needed); sub-cache and handler-replaced trace are dev-only (gated on the trace goog-define).
notes:
Spec updated in PR #39. New scope:
- (rf/app-schemas frame-id) keyword-arity sugar over the existing opts-map arity.
- Plus the original snapshot-of / sub-cache / :rf.registry/handler-replaced.
Note: :dispatch-id / :parent-dispatch-id (Spec 009) and :origin opt (Spec 002) are reserved for the trace-buffer agent (rf2-smee) since they live on the trace event envelope.
close_reason: PR #45 merged. :frame opt on app-schemas accepted for forward-compat but doesn't narrow (v1 schema registry is process-global).

title: Spec 009: document :rf.error/effect-map-shape category (rf2-ooc5 follow-up)
description:
rf2-ooc5 introduced :rf.error/effect-map-shape as the structured trace that fires when a reg-event-fx handler returns a top-level key other than :db or :fx (per Spec migration M-8).

Spec 009 §Error categories (initial set) does not currently list this category. Add a row:

| :rf.error/effect-map-shape | A reg-event-fx handler returned a top-level key other than :db / :fx (per MIGRATION §M-8). | :failing-id (event-id), :event, :offending-key, :value |

Default recovery: :logged-and-skipped (the offending key is dropped; :db and :fx still apply, sibling fx still walk).

Style rubric example: "Effect-map for \`:cart/save\` returned top-level key \`:dispatch\`; only \`:db\` and \`:fx\` are allowed at the top level — wrap as \`:fx [[:dispatch event]]\`."

Implementation already lives at re-frame.events/police-effect-map-shape! and re-frame.events/fx-handler->interceptor in implementation/src/re_frame/events.cljc; covered by re-frame.fx-test/legacy-effect-map-key-is-policed and multiple-legacy-effect-map-keys-each-emit-a-trace in implementation/test/re_frame/fx_test.clj.
close_reason: PR #47 merged.

title: CLJS test fixtures should snapshot/restore the registrar uniformly
description:
Several CLJS test ns fixtures call `(registrar/clear-all!)` which is
fundamentally hostile to CLJS test isolation: CLJS has no runtime
`(require :reload)` and the example namespaces (e.g. nine-states.core,
plus shipped framework nses like re-frame.routing) register their
handlers ONCE at namespace-load time. Once cleared, those
registrations cannot be reinstated for downstream tests.

rf2-p8g8's PR added a save/restore pattern to four CLJS test fixtures:
  - cross_spec_cljs_test.cljs
  - machines_cljs_test.cljs
  - runtime_cljs_test.cljs
  - schemas_cljs_test.cljs

so that each test's `clear-all!` is bracketed by an outer
@registrar/kind->id->metadata snapshot + reset on the way out.

This follow-up is to:

1. Decide whether this save/restore pattern should become the canonical
   CLJS test-fixture shape (e.g. by lifting it into a shared
   `re-frame.test-utils` ns) so future tests don't repeat the boilerplate
   and don't accidentally re-introduce the cross-test pollution.

2. Audit the JVM test fixtures for symmetry — JVM uses
   `(require ... :reload)` to resurrect framework registrations after
   `clear-all!`, but example apps (examples/nine_states/core.cljs is
   .cljs-only; others may have .cljc that JVM tests import) might face
   the same issue.

3. Consider providing a registrar API like
   `clear-keep-defaults!` / `with-fresh-registrar` so test authors have
   a positive API rather than relying on the side-effecting save/reset
   pattern.

Filed as the follow-up to rf2-p8g8 per the bead's "If the root cause
turns out to be a fixable test-fixture bug ... file a `bd create`
follow-up" guidance.
close_reason: PR #38 merged.

title: Drain depth-exceeded: spec/impl gap on atomic rollback of failing handler's :db
description: Bead rf2-yqpg's brief asks the drain-depth-exceeded test to assert 'atomic rollback (failing handler's :db is reverted)'. The current router (implementation/src/re_frame/router.cljc drain! lines 182-205) does NOT roll back :db — when the depth bound trips, it emits :rf.error/drain-depth-exceeded and clears the queue, but every previously-committed :db (including the deepest event before the bound) is preserved. The drain_test.clj added in rf2-yqpg therefore asserts what the impl actually does (queue cleared, scheduled? reset) and pins the structured error tags. Spec 002 §Run-to-completion §Rules rule 3 only specifies the error envelope — no rollback is called out. Decide: (a) tighten the spec to match impl (no rollback claim) and update the rf2-yqpg brief, OR (b) add rollback to drain! plus a regression test. Filed during rf2-yqpg implementation per CLAUDE.md 'file beads for spec gaps'.

title: Tests: framework init/boot lifecycle — init!, ensure-default-frame, install-adapter contract, dispose-adapter
description:
Framework boot today is exercised transitively in every test (via reset-runtime fixtures). No dedicated coverage of:

- init! idempotence: calling twice doesn't double-install adapter or duplicate :rf/default frame.
- install-adapter! semantics: second call without dispose raises :rf.error/adapter-already-installed.
- dispose-adapter!: tears down + clears slot; subsequent install! works.
- ensure-default-frame: creates :rf/default if absent; no-op if present.
- Adapter swap: dispose then install a different adapter (plain-atom -> Reagent or vice versa) preserves nothing across the boundary.

Deliverable: NEW file implementation/test/re_frame/boot_test.clj.
close_reason: PR #20 merged.

title: Bug: routing-cljs-test fails when run alongside other CLJS tests
description:
Discovered while extracting PR #14. The CLJS routing tests landed via PR #13 (rf2-hh37) — the agent's report said they pass — but on the current main with all other CLJS tests in scope, both routing-cljs-test deftests fail with multiple assertion failures:

- routing-handle-url-change-cljs (5 failures at lines 71, 74, 77, 82, 85)
- routing-frame-provider-routing-cljs (6 failures at lines 114, 116, 118, 120, 125, 128)

Plus the long-standing nine-states-runs-end-to-end error.

Likely cross-test global-state pollution: the routing tests probably depend on registrar state that another test ns mutates / wipes. The CLJS test fixture (reset-runtime in runtime_cljs_test.cljs) calls registrar/clear-all! and re-loads only re-frame.routing and re-frame.ssr — if some other ns's state isn't restored after wipe, downstream routing tests see a partially-init'd registry.

Investigation: run each test ns in isolation to confirm individual passes; identify which ns's pollution is causing the regression in shared run.
notes:
Fix landed in PR #29: https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/29

Removed registrar/clear-all! from cross_spec_cljs_test and machines_cljs_test reset-runtime fixtures. Both fixtures use unique ids per test, so the wipe was unnecessary for in-file isolation, and it was destroying routing.cljc's ns-load framework registrations (which CLJS can't re-load). All 54 CLJS tests now pass (was 23 failures).

title: Bug: flows/last-inputs (private dirty-check atom) leaks across smoke tests
description:
Discovered by the rf2-1cli agent. The flows namespace has a private atom flows/last-inputs that holds {[frame-id flow-id] -> last-input-vector} for dirty-check. The existing reset-runtime fixture in smoke_test.clj doesn't reset this atom, so flow registrations in test ns A can leak the last-inputs into test ns B that registers a same-named flow against the same frame.

Symptom: a flow's body fn doesn't fire on its first registration in a new test if a prior test left a stale entry at [:rf/default :flow-id] that matches the new test's initial app-db state by =.

Fix: in implementation/test/re_frame/smoke_test.clj's reset-runtime, after registrar/clear-all!, also reset the flows private state. The agent's PR #10 flows_test.clj does this for its own ns; the existing smoke fixture should do the same so future tests don't cross-pollute.

Discovered-from: rf2-1cli.
close_reason: smoke_test.clj reset-runtime now resets re-frame.flows/last-inputs (matching the pattern in flows_test.clj) so dirty-check state can't leak across deftests.

title: Bug: :rf.machine.timer/stale-after not emitted when machine has moved out of the after-bearing state
description:
Discovered by the rf2-pc82 agent and confirmed by rf2-2xg9. pick-after-transition only emits the :rf.machine.timer/stale-after trace event when the leaf state STILL contains an :after table at the carried delay. The canonical staleness scenario from after-stale-detection.edn — machine schedules an :after timer in state A, transitions to state B (no :after), the late timer fires — silently swallows: no trace, no transition, no observable signal.

Spec 009 §Trace events documents :rf.machine.timer/stale-after as the canonical signal for this case. The runtime correctly does not transition (good — that's the stale-suppression contract), but does not emit the trace, so observers can't tell whether the timer was suppressed or never fired.

Fix: in machines.cljc's pick-after-transition, emit :rf.machine.timer/stale-after even when the current state has no :after table (any non-matching epoch on a synthetic timer event is a stale carry, by definition).

Discovered-from: rf2-pc82, rf2-2xg9.
close_reason: PR #23 merged.

title: Conformance: claim :flow/* capabilities so the new flow fixtures actually run
description:
Discovered by the rf2-1cli agent. The conformance runner has a manifest of claimed capabilities (per Spec 005 §Capability matrix idiom, generalised). The new flow-* fixtures from PR #10 load but are SKIPPED because the runner does not currently claim :flow/recompute-on-input, :flow/multi-input-topo, :flow/value-equal-noop, :flow/toggle-via-fx, :flow/hot-reload-preserves.

Add the :flow/* capability list to the runner's claimed set in implementation/test/re_frame/conformance_test.clj (or wherever the manifest lives).

Discovered-from: rf2-1cli.
close_reason: Conformance runner now claims :flow/basic, :flow/topo, :flow/dirty-check, :flow/toggle, :flow/hot-reload — all 5 flow-*.edn fixtures move from skipped to runnable. Follow-up rf2-wdhi tracks the impl bug those fixtures expose.

title: Spec/impl divergence: machine action exceptions surface as :rf.error/handler-exception not :rf.error/machine-action-exception
description:
Discovered by the rf2-o8qw cross-spec-tests agent.

docs/specification/Cross-Spec-Interactions.md cites :rf.error/machine-action-exception as the trace operation emitted when a machine action throws. The current implementation surfaces these exceptions via the standard :rf.error/handler-exception path (because machine actions are reg-event-fx handlers under the hood). The cross_spec_cljs_test.cljs tests for cases #11 and #17 verify the actually-emitted operation rather than the documented one.

Two ways to resolve:

1. **Update the spec** to acknowledge that machine action exceptions ride the generic :rf.error/handler-exception route (with :tags carrying machine-id, action-id, and state-path). Simpler; matches reality.
2. **Update the impl** to wrap action invocations and emit a more specific :rf.error/machine-action-exception with machine-scoped detail. Better diagnostics, more code, more test churn.

P3: it's correctness-of-documentation, not correctness-of-runtime. Pick when the trace-stream completeness work (rf2-91tl) lands and we have a fuller view of the trace surface.

Discovered-from: rf2-o8qw.

title: Spec 009 op-type vocabulary mismatch: many documented ops never emitted
description:
Spec 009 §:op-type vocabulary documents these op-types / operations as part of the trace contract, but the implementation never emits them:

- :sub/run and :sub/create — sub recompute / materialisation
- :render — view render
- :raf — animation frame
- :reagent/quiescent — post-render hook
- :sync — dispatch-sync wrapper
- ::fsm-trigger — router state-machine trigger
- :frame/reset — distinct from :frame/destroyed + :frame/created
- :rf.machine.lifecycle/created / :rf.machine.lifecycle/destroyed
- :rf.machine/event-received
- :rf.machine/snapshot-updated
- :route.url/fragment-changed (impl emits :rf.route/url-changed instead — name mismatch)
- :rf.registry/handler-registered (impl only emits :rf.registry/handler-replaced)
- :rf.registry/handler-cleared

Discovered while writing rf2-91tl trace-stream-completeness test. The test asserts the documented op fires; for these ops it asserts via (is (some ...) "expected op never emitted — see THIS BEAD")  so the regression surface stays visible. Either the spec needs to be tightened (drop the unimplemented ops) or the implementation needs the missing emits.

## Context
discovered-from:rf2-91tl
close_reason: PR #36 merged.

title: Tests: trace-stream completeness (Spec 009) — every documented operation emits at the right moment
description: Spec 009 enumerates ~25 trace operations (:event/dispatched, :event/handled, :sub/recomputed, :sub/disposed, :fx/applied, :fx/skipped-on-platform, :machine/transitioned, :machine/always-microstep, :rf.error/*, etc.). Today only error operations are exercised by the error/* fixtures. Need a single comprehensive smoke test that registers a trace listener, runs a representative dispatch flow that should emit each documented op, and asserts the canonical event shape per op. Plus the listener API: register, removal, multiple listeners, listener exception isolation. JVM smoke is sufficient; the trace stream is substrate-independent.
notes: PR: https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/6
close_reason: PR #6 merged.

title: Tests: per-pattern smoke suite (Forms, Boot, RemoteData, WebSocket, StaleDetection, LongRunningWork)
description: Each Pattern-*.md doc proposes a canonical shape (slice keys, lifecycle events, status enum). Tests today exercise these only transitively via examples_test.clj. A dedicated per-pattern smoke test catches drift between pattern doc and implementation. Pattern-Forms: the form slice + 7-event lifecycle + per-field touched/error. Pattern-Boot: chained-async sequence + boot machine variant + SSR handoff. Pattern-RemoteData: the 5-key lifecycle slice (:status :data :error :loaded-at :attempt). Pattern-WebSocket: the connection state machine. Pattern-StaleDetection: epoch capture + carry + suppress. Pattern-LongRunningWork: chunked machine + :after 0 yield + progress reads. New file implementation/test/re_frame/pattern_smoke_test.clj recommended; one deftest per pattern.
close_reason: PR #8 merged.

title: Conformance DSL: :fx op doesn't eval-value its args (reflection forms come through literally)
description:
**Discovered by the rf2-3nqu fixture-authoring agent.** When writing a fixture that needs an action to emit an :fx pair whose argument depends on prior state, the natural form is something like:

    {:op :fx :fx [:destroy-machine [:get [:child-id]]]}

so the actor-id is read from the fixture's recorded child-id, not hardcoded. The conformance interpreter's realise-machine-handlers `:fx` op does not pass the second argument through `eval-value`, so the reflection form `[:get [:child-id]]` arrives at the runtime literally (a vector, not the resolved value).

**Workaround used in destroy-clears-snapshot.edn:** hardcode the actor-id (`:worker#1`) in the action body. The fixture's docstring flags the limitation.

**Suggested fix:** apply `eval-value` to the second argument of `:fx` in the realise-machine-handlers code path, mirroring the treatment in other `:set`/`:dispatch` ops. This would let fixtures express data-flow naturally without per-fixture workarounds.

**Touchpoint:** `implementation/src/re_frame/conformance.cljc`'s realise-machine-handlers function (search `:fx`).

**Discovered-from:** rf2-3nqu.
close_reason: PR #35 merged.

title: Tests: CLJS-side coverage gap for hierarchical / always / after / invoke machines under Reagent
description:
**Why:** CLJS tests currently exercise only the flat machine-transition path (`machine-transition-cljs` in `runtime_cljs_test.cljs`) and the nine-states pattern. Hierarchical states, eventless `:always` transitions, delayed `:after` transitions, and declarative `:invoke` are all CLJS-claimed capabilities but only tested on the JVM (via fixtures and smoke tests).

**Risk:** Reagent-adapter-specific issues (reaction propagation timing, hot-reload of machine handlers, drain interaction with rAF batching) won't surface until a real app exercises them.
